question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any work their Department has undertaken in the last 12 months to address issues emerging from the UK COVID-19 Inquiry, including in relation to contingency planning.
answer_text: During the past 12 months work has continued to strengthen resilience and preparedness arrangements in NI.  We, as an Executive, considered Civil Contingencies arrangements on 29 February 2024. The “NI Civil Contingencies Framework – Building Resilience Together”, originally published in July 2021, sets out the Northern Ireland arrangements for civil emergencies and identifies the processes involved in responding to and recovering from an emergency. The Framework, which brought together existing protocols and guidance, is regularly reviewed, with the most recent review being carried out in 2023. The Covid-19 Inquiry Modular Recommendations and lessons learned steering group, with representation from all NICS departments, was established in 2024 to progress recommendations arising from the Covid-19 Inquiry and implement identified lessons learned.The Executive Response to the Covid-19 Inquiry: Resilience and Preparedness (Module 1) Report, published on 16 January 2025, provides further detail on actions that have been taken since the Covid-19 Pandemic and outlines how we plan to implement the recommendations contained in Module 1 Report.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any assessment being made of their Department's performance in terms of not yet appointing a Commissioner for Public Appointments, a year on from the restoration of the institutions.
answer_text: We are pleased to report that the competition to appoint a Commissioner for Public Appointments for NI (CPANI) launched on 10 March.A Commissioner will be appointed as soon as possible, following the conclusion of the appointment process.Officials will conduct an evaluation of the process once complete.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how their Department is monitoring changes to the European Union Racial Equality Directive, to ensure obligations under Article 2 of the Windsor Framework are maintained.
answer_text: The Executive Office (TEO) uses the EU Legislation and Information Tracking Service (EULITS), which is updated weekly by the EU to monitor equality directives.In addition, The Executive Office coordinates an Article 2 Interdepartmental Working Group with representatives from other Departments. This supports the updating, tracking and coordination on the relevant Directives, including the Race Equality Directive 2000/43/EC.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any steps their Department took during 2024 to implement the Independent Reporting Commission recommendations on providing resources to end paramilitarism.
answer_text: We welcome the Independent Reporting Commission’s sixth report and positive comments in relation to the impact the Communities in Transition (CIT) programme, led by our Department, is having on ending paramilitarism.Under the Executive’s Programme for Paramilitarism and Organised Crime CIT was awarded £2.3m for the year 2024/25 which was supplemented in year by a further £1.5m from monitoring rounds following a bid from our Department. As a result, CIT is delivering 29 projects worth £3.8m across a range of issues in 8 designated areas.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any work their Department has undertaken in relation to providing for intersectionality within equality legislation.
answer_text: The issue of intersectionality was recognised by the Executive in the Racial Equality Strategy 2015-2025 and considered as part of the review of the Race Relations (NI) Order 1997.Equality Legislation is currently comprised of separate acts which provide protection against discrimination on the grounds of age, disability, race, religion and political opinion, sex and sexual orientation. Discrimination claims may be brought on the basis of more than one characteristic protected by existing equality legislation.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when their Department last reviewed the European Union Directive on Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence, with a view to ensuring there are no gaps in equality protections.
answer_text: EU Directive 2024/1760, to foster sustainable and responsible corporate behaviour in companies’ operations and across their global value chains, has no impact on any of the six WF Annex 1 directives.Anti-discrimination legislation is currently comprised of separate Acts which provide protection against discrimination on the grounds of age, disability, race, religion and political opinion, sex and sexual orientation.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any changes there have been to European Union equality law since 1 January 2021 which are still outstanding for implementation in law in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: We are advised that Article 2 of the Windsor Framework requires that the EU Standards for Equality Bodies, and the Pay Transparency Directives are transposed here by July 2026. This matter will be considered in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any training provided across the Northern Ireland Civil Service on the effective embedding of Article 2 of the Windsor Framework policy and processes.
answer_text: Officials, with line manager support, identify their training needs and where a gap is identified they undertake the necessary learning. In the case of Windsor Framework Article 2 this takes the form of a training webinar which is accessible on all departmental intranets.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, further to my recent written correspondence and my visit to Newforge Sports Complex and Event Hub, for an update on plans to transfer surplus adjacent land owned by his Department to the complex for potential use as an additional sports facility.
answer_text: My Department has now completed the initial phase of a Strategic Review of the Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute’s Land and Property Holdings. This has helped inform considerations around the lands at Newforge and discussions are ongoing between officials from my Department, the Newforge Community Development Trust and, indeed, other interested parties about the potential next steps for the DAERA lands at Newforge. These discussions are being taken forward on a ‘without prejudice’ basis and any potential disposal or lease of the lands in the future will follow due process.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the work their Department is currently undertaking to prepare for the extension of age as a category in law concerning discrimination in the provision of goods and services.
answer_text: No one in our society should be discriminated against because of their age. Advice on this issue is currently under review.Junior Ministers met with Claire Sugden MLA to discuss her proposed Age Goods, Facilities and Services Bill and the challenges in bringing forward legislation on this issue. Development of any age discrimination laws will require careful consideration and work to inform their scope, as well as required exemptions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the Belfast City Council's Segregation and the Environment Breaking Down Barriers research report, which highlighted the opportunity to address climate change by reducing segregation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any analysis their Department has undertaken of the EU Directive on combating violence against women and domestic violence.
answer_text: As part of the development of the Strategic Framework a number of impact assessments were undertaken. Officials consider international good practice, including this Directive, in developing our programme of work.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the contingencies in place to ensure that asylum accommodation is adequate for families with children who have disabilities.
answer_text: The provision of accommodation for asylum seekers is solely a matter for Home Office and their contractor Mears Housing.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the provision of a permanent Crisis Fund, in line with the NI Human Rights Commission's Annual Statement 2024.
answer_text: We recognise the importance of the Crisis Fund as it provides support to some of the most vulnerable within our society at risk of destitution.  Subject to budget, we have agreed to initiate procurement for the Crisis Fund administrator for the 2025/26 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any barriers that exist to the full implementation of the actions identified by the Expert Panel on Educational Underachievement, in line with the Northern Ireland Human Rights Commission's Annual Statement.
answer_text: With reference to the recommendation contained in the NIHRC’s Annual Statement, I can confirm that my Department is ensuring that ‘…every effort is made to use all resources at its disposal to implement the Expert Panel’s findings.’ Funding uncertainty during 2023/24 and 2024/25 for education continues to impact on the extent to which the 47 actions for change identified within the report can be delivered. However there have been some positive signs of progress and details are published in A Fair Start Progress Report 6.The barriers to full implementation of the 47 actions are financial and not due to a lack of commitment from my Department.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any changes their Department will make by the end of the 2024-25 financial year on how it monitors levels of period poverty.
answer_text: The Department has no responsibility for monitoring levels of period poverty.  However, under section 1 of the Period Products (Free Provision) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, TEO, in partnership with Libraries NI, are providing period products throughout the Libraries NI network of 96 static and 11 mobile libraries. Free products have been accessible from May 2024 to anyone who needs to use them regardless of economic status. To date over one million items have been collected from Library premises.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any work undertaken during 2024 to implement proposals and recommendations in the report of the Commission on Flags, Identity, Culture and Tradition in relation to parades and protests.
answer_text: The recommendations included in the FICT Report are being taken forward as part of the T:BUC Review.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any steps their Department took during 2024 to monitor levels of period poverty.
answer_text: TEO, in partnership with Libraries NI, are providing period products throughout the Libraries NI network of 96 static and 11 mobile libraries. Free products have been accessible from May 2024 to anyone who needs to use them regardless of economic status.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he conducted during 2024 with representative organisations of refugees and people seeking asylum in relation to adequate accommodation.
answer_text: The Executive Office (TEO) is the strategic lead for Refugee resettlement and asylum seekers in Northern Ireland.  My Department continues to work with the NIHE and TEO, as appropriate, in relation to accommodation for refugees. The Home Office is responsible for accommodation for asylum seekers.  If asylum seekers are granted refugee status or leave to remain, they become eligible to apply for social housing through the Northern Ireland Housing Executive and they are assessed on their housing need under the Housing Selection Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps his Department took during 2024 to deliver specialised support services for victims and survivors of conversion therapy practices.
answer_text: My department did not take any steps to deliver specialised support services for victims and survivors of conversion therapy practices in 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps he took during 2024 to provide the necessary financial support, debt relief and education to ensure people are not at risk from illegal lenders.
answer_text: Tackling illegal money lending spans the remit of several departments and requires a co-ordinated approach.	My Department is responsible for financial wellbeing policy, including debt advice, in Northern Ireland and provides funding to Advice NI and its affiliated local advice centres to deliver the NI Debt Advice Service, which supports victims of illegal money lending by offering free, independent debt advice and signposting.		The Discretionary Support service provides a fast and responsive means of delivering short term financial support to assist with day-to-day living expenses if a person is in an extreme, exceptional or crisis situation. Discretionary Support can offer help towards short-term living expenses or household items via an interest free loan or a non-repayable grant to people experiencing financial difficulties, provided a person meets the eligibility criteria. 	  My officials continue to work with colleagues in The Executive Programme on Paramilitarism & Organised Crime, who raise awareness of the impact of illegal money lending via its ‘Ending the Harm’ campaign.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any action his Department took during 2024 to implement recommendations of the UN Committee on the Rights of the Child in relation to children’s rights to rest, leisure and recreation, particularly with reference to provision of sports facilities and instruction.
answer_text: The statutory responsibility for securing the provision of adequate local facilities for recreational, social, physical and cultural activities, including provision for children’s recreation, lies with the relevant local authority, in accordance with Article 10 of the Recreation and Youth Service (Northern Ireland) Order 1986.The NI Strategy for Sport and Physical Activity - Active Living has key themes to increase participation opportunities in sport and physical activity for everyone, including children, and also a key theme on the importance of developing/improving sporting facilities. These are key areas for my Department and in 2024/25 £2million exchequer funding has been invested in these areas via Sport NI.  Additionally, the Neighbourhood Renewal programme delivers a range of area-based initiatives designed to address spatial concentrations of deprivation.  It does not specifically address children’s leisure and recreation although a number of funded projects provide personal development though sporting activities across a wide range of ages, including children and young people:Community Sports Network – grant funding in 2024/25 of £44,708.73.Belfast Activity Centre – grant funding in 2024/25 of £132,984.27.The two figures above are the total grant funding to each organisation.  It is not possible to breakdown funding solely related to children’s leisure and recreation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action his Department is taking in relation to tackling modern slavery and human trafficking as per the Northern Ireland Human Rights Commission's Annual Statement 2024, particularly with regard to promoting good job opportunities to enable women to become less vulnerable to sex for rent.
answer_text: Modern slavery is the policy remit of Department of Justice.  I am committed to updating the employment law framework in this mandate to increase the number of people working in good jobs.  I will do this by focussing on four key themes of a good job:Terms of employment;Pay and benefits;Employee voice; and Worklife balance.By modernising and strengthening the law in these four areas we can help improve job security for those accessing the workforce.My department also provides a range of programmes designed to support individuals who face barriers to employment with the aim of developing a flexible skills system that will work for everyone, no matter what their background, education or personal circumstances. This is a key feature of the Skills Fund that I launched in July with an investment of almost £12m, facilitating skills interventions such as ‘Step Up’ and ‘Skill Up’.  We also have all age Apprenticeships in place now, with an Apprenticeship Challenge Fund in place to help those from under-represented groups.I also recognise the relationship between the provision of adequate childcare support and the ability for parents to participate in education and employment.  To this end, £740k of the Skills Fund was allocated this year to support pathways into the childcare sector, helping ensure an adequate supply of workers into this critically important sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on when the Unauthorised Encampments (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 will be repealed.
answer_text: There are no plans to repeal the Unauthorised Encampments (Northern Ireland) Order 2005.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the provision of regulations to implement the Health and Social Care (Control of Data Processing) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care (Control of Data Processing) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 Act received Royal Ascent on 11th April 2016. Following unavoidable interruptions, planning resumed in 2022 for the introduction of the required regulations to enable the sharing of identifiable health data for secondary usages, beyond the limited and specific conditions available at present. The process to draft regulations included a full review of the Act (2016) with guidance from the Department Solicitors Office. This review identified a substantive issue in the wording of the 2016 Act, regarding the scope of applicable data which could have encroached on the original intent of the legislation.My department is currently considering which legislative vehicle may be most suitable to bring forward required amendments to the original Act (2016), before addressing the provision of regulations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for a detailed timescale for the introduction of Special Educational Needs Regulations.
answer_text: My officials are currently finalising the new Special Educational Needs (SEN) Framework, which incorporates the new SEN Regulations and the associated statutory Code of Practice.  Once all the necessary approvals are in place they will be presented to the Education Committee for scrutiny and will then commence their legislative journey through the Northern Ireland Assembly.It is currently my intention to bring forward the revised SEN Regulations to the Executive for consideration before the end of this mandate.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will publish a list of the UK Covid-19 Inquiry recommendations that they have (i) accepted in full; and (ii) accepted only in part.
answer_text: The Covid-19 Inquiry: Resilience and Preparedness (Module 1) Report Executive Response, published on 16 January 2025, outlines the Executive’s position on each recommendation and the steps being taken to implement these. A summary of recommendations is contained within the table below.RecommendationResponseA simplified structure for whole-system civil emergency preparedness and resilienceAcceptedCabinet Office leadership for whole-system civil emergencies in the UKAccepted in principleA better approach to risk assessmentAcceptedA UK-Wide whole-system civil emergency strategyAccepted in principleData and research for future pandemicsAcceptedA regular UK-wide pandemic response exerciseAcceptedPublication of findings and lessons from civil emergency exercisesAcceptedPublished reports on whole-system civil emergency preparedness and resilienceAcceptedRegular use of red teamsAccepted in principle A UK-wide independent statutory bode for whole-system civil emergency preparedness and resiliencePartially accepted

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will publish a more detailed response to the UK Covid-19 Inquiry's findings thus far.
answer_text: The Covid-19 Inquiry Resilience and Preparedness (Module 1) Report Executive Response, published on 16 January 2025, represents the first 6-month progress report on implementation of the recommendations that UK Covid Inquiry recommended. The Executive response highlights positive progress in a number of areas since Module 1 hearings finished. Work continues on implementing recommendations and the Next Steps section of our response provides detail on Actions that will be taken forward over the course of implementation.   The Executive will consider and publish a further progress report against these Actions in July 2025.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any learning there has been from implementing the recommendations of the Renewable Heat Incentive Inquiry towards implementing the recommendations of the UK Covid-19 Inquiry.
answer_text: All recommendations arising from the Covid Inquiry Module 1 Report have been considered fully and responded to.  Deliverable actions and timescales have been identified to address the issues raised.  The Executive response was approved at the Executive meeting on 9 January 2025. A cross-departmental steering group is in place to monitor delivery and report to the Executive.  The Executive will publish a further update on progress in July 2025, in line with the requirements set out by the Chair of the Inquiry.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the timescale under which recommendations published thus far by the UK Covid-19 Inquiry will be implemented.
answer_text: The Covid-19 Inquiry: Resilience and Preparedness (Module 1) Report Executive Response, published on 16 January 2025, outlines the Executive response to the recommendations made by the UK Covid-19 Inquiry in the Module 1 Report.The Executive response provides details on Next Steps stage regarding the continued implementation of the recommendations.   The Executive will consider and publish a further report on the implementation of recommendation in July 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline any work undertaken by her Department during 2024 to prevent forced marriages.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any proposals he has brought forward to ensure that Northern Ireland is involved in UK-wide clinical trials.
answer_text: Infrastructure to support Northern Ireland involvement in UK-wide clinical trials in funded through the HSC Research and Development Division in the Public Health Agency.The Northern Ireland Clinical Research Network (NICRN) provides infrastructure that allows high-quality clinical research to take place across all Health and Social Care (HSC) structures, so that patients can benefit from new and better treatments. Many of the trials supported by the NICRN are UK-wide. Current and past examples include: REMAP-CAP, PANORAMIC, PHOSP-COVID.The Northern Ireland Cancer Trials Network (NICTN) co-ordinates and promotes cancer clinical trials, and runs the full range of first-in-human phase I to phase IV trials, along with genetic epidemiology, questionnaire, quality of life, translational and other high quality studies. Many of these trials are UK-wide. The current portfolio list can be viewed at https://cancertrials.hscni.net/portfolio/portfolio-open-trials/.The Northern Ireland Clinical Trials Unit (NICTU) is a UK Clinical Research Collaboration (UKCRC) registered Clinical Trials Unit based in Belfast. The NICTU provides specialist advice, support and training for investigators undertaking research within or of value to the HSC. In particular NICTU supports and co-ordinates multi-centre UK-wide clinical trials. Their current portfolio of studies can be accessed at https://nictu.hscni.net/portfolio/.In addition, representatives of the HSC R&D Division and the Chief Scientific Advisor participate in a wide range of UK networks and structures which support Northern Ireland participation in UK wide clinical trials.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the main differences between his three-year plan for elective care and that being implemented by the UK Government in England, given the particular challenges in Northern Ireland including inefficiencies in hospital throughput and longer waiting lists.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail a timescale for the re-establishment of the local gender identity services.
answer_text: My Department has been working together with the Belfast HSC Trust to address the issues of waiting times, resources, and to develop a new model of care for Gender Identity Services for transgender people living in NI.  As part of this process, a Gender Identity Service Pathway Review Group was established and Chaired by the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG), with membership on the group including, the Public Health Agency, the Patient Client Council, the Royal College of General Practitioners and officials from Policy in my Department. The work of the Pathway Review Group began in September 2019.  There was significant engagement undertaken to help inform the review, notwithstanding the obvious challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic. A Gender Identity Liaison Panel, which sits under the main Pathway Review Group, was also established to enable those with lived experience to provide input to the review. On completion of the review, my officials have continued  to  work closely and collaboratively with the Belfast HSC Trust, the Gender Identity Review Group and Gender Identity Liaison Group to develop a business case for the first phase of service expansion, which identifies the extra resources required to enhance the capacity of the services to meet the growing demand and includes additionality to address the implications of the Cass Review report. Funding of around £740k will be required recurrently to fund the service development.  The business case details the extra medical and nursing resources required, including additional endocrinology provision. General Practitioner shared care arrangement are currently being explored for Phase 2 of this service development. The business case was submitted for consideration on 4th November 2024. My officials are working closely with Belfast Trust as part of the necessary approval process.   My officials are working at pace to finalise the business case, and it is anticipated that process will be concluded later this month.I am committed to improving wider gender identity service provision for children, young people and adults in NI. Despite the very challenging financial position facing my Department, I have advised my officials that I will prioritise the development of this service to ensure the appropriate advice and support is available to individuals and their families.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his plans to prioritise transgender healthcare through the re-establishment of local gender identity services.
answer_text: My Department has been working together with the Belfast HSC Trust to address the issues of waiting times, resources, and to develop a new model of care for Gender Identity Services for transgender people living in NI. question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline his Department's plans to increase post-primary places in South Belfast to meet current and projected future demands.

To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has brought forward any proposals, either to the Executive or UK Government, in relation to expanding Freedom of Information legislation to include organisations in receipt of public money.
answer_text: Schedule 1 of the Freedom of Information Act 2000 already includes a wide range of organisations in receipt of public money.  I have not brought forward any proposals to either the Executive or the UK Government in relation to expanding Freedom of Information legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps he took during 2024 to explore the use of Shared Island funding to improve facilities for the playing of team sports.
answer_text: I have not corresponded with the Irish government in relation to the Shared Island Fund.As I stated in my response to AQW 6098/22-27, I continue to look forward to working with the widest range of funding partners where there is an opportunity to improve investment in communities, including in sporting facilities in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps he is taking to improve the provision of hockey pitches in the west of Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have recently accepted an invitation to meet with Marc Scott, Chief Executive of Ulster Hockey, and look forward to meeting him in the near future to discuss matters relating to hockey in Northern Ireland.  In terms of the provision of hockey pitches in the west of Northern Ireland, I would refer you to Article 10 of the Recreation and Youth Service (Northern Ireland) Order 1986, the statutory responsibility for the provision of facilities for recreational, sport, physical and cultural activities, including hockey pitches, lies with the relevant local authority for each area.I understand that Sport NI, an arm’s length body of the Department, is undertaking an infrastructure review of sporting and physical activity facilities across Northern Ireland. When completed it is anticipated the results of this survey will be shared with Councils and the sports sector and may help identify areas where provision of facilities can be improved.Finally, subject to funding, I hope to announce the reopening of the Your School Your Club initiative for 2025-2026 in the next few months.  The purpose of the initiative is to promote community use of school sports facilities outside of normal school hours. Investment is disbursed to revitalise existing school sports facilities or to develop new and additional provision on school sites whilst ensuring access and use for local community users, including hockey clubs.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how long preparations to start the recruitment process for a new Commissioner for Public Appointments will take.
answer_text: We are pleased to report that the competition to appoint a Commissioner for Public Appointments for NI (CPANI) is well progressed and a joint decision on the successful candidate has been made. A formal announcement will be made as soon as possible, following the conclusion of the appointment process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to seeking to bring Housing Associations which receive public money under Freedom of Information legislation.
answer_text: I have no plans to bring Housing Associations which receive public money under Freedom of Information legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on any plans to address concerns around dog breeding establishments.
answer_text: I am undertaking a series of animal welfare meetings with key stakeholders to listen to their views on this matter. I most recently met with the Northern Ireland Companion Animal Welfare Group in December with further meetings planned with local councils and the PSNI this week. The outcome of this engagement will be an Animal Welfare Pathway which will detail my key commitments and objectives in the area of animal welfare until the end of the mandate. I have already expressed my desire, within this pathway of actions, to create an expert group to review current dog breeding regulations. It would be my intention that this group will examine the need for reform and bring forward recommendations for my consideration. If legislative deficiencies are identified in the course of this review, I am, of course, determined that appropriate resolutions are advanced.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how long preparations to start the recruitment process for a new Commissioner for Public Appointments will take.
answer_text: Preparations to appoint a Commissioner for Public Appointments are already well progressed, and the competition will be launched in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, excluding the establishment of a strategic oversight group, to detail any actions that will be taken to reduce traffic congestion in Belfast city centre before 21 December 2024.
answer_text: The ongoing work in and around Belfast represents a significant investment, which will ultimately improve and enhance the cityscape, however I fully appreciate that this work is causing delay and frustration for motorists travelling through or to the city centre.To minimise the impact of traffic congestion in the run up to Christmas, I recently announced a pause of non-essential roadworks on key routes into Belfast, which commenced on Monday 25th November. This has been carefully planned to ensure that only the most urgent works are carried out on the identified arterial routes into the city centre between 25th November and 2nd January.My Department continues to consider what more can be done to ease congestion at peak times and this work is ongoing.  Considerations have included traffic management interventions, real-time monitoring to improve the flow of public transport, and advice to commuters and others coming into the city centre on the alternative transport and travel options available to them.  Interventions that have been completed, or which are planned, include: adjustment and optimisation of traffic signals; engagement with PSNI to increase visibility and engagement to improve compliance with yellow box restrictions; P&R service enhancement, signage and messaging; and increased use of TrafficwatchNI X account providing up to date messages to drivers.Mitigating the congestion issues will need a huge collective approach, with everyone considering how they can play a part to help everyone better share out the limited road space available in a smarter way.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister who is accountable for the outcomes of the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme.
answer_text: The North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme (NBSGRP) is delivered by the Community Relations Council (CRC) on behalf of The Executive Office (TEO).

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme is currently evaluated.
answer_text: The North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme (NBSGRP) is evaluated annually by the Community Relations Council, who are responsible for the delivery of the programme.Periodic independent evaluations are also conducted, with the most recent being in 2022 carried out by Department of Finance’s Business Consultancy Services. The finding of this review confirms that the programme has been successful in achieving its intended aims of improving relations between and within communities in North Belfast; providing local solutions to local good relations issues; and contributing to progress the four key priorities in the T:BUC Strategy of Children & Young People, Shared Community, Safe Community and Cultural Expression.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any evaluations of the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme that have been published since the 2018-19 financial year.
answer_text: An annual update on the programme is included in the T:BUC Annual Report. Officials are currently finalising T:BUC Annual Reports for our consideration ahead of publication.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the organisations to which the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme is open.
answer_text: Ardoyne Youth Providers ForumAshton Community Trust Ballysillan Community Forum Cliftonville Community Regeneration Forum Greater Whitewell Community Surgery Intercomm Ligoniel Improvement Association Mount Vernon Community Development ForumShankill Women’s Centre The Vine Centre

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of rising costs due to changes to National Insurance announced in the Autumn Budget 2024, to detail any new plans he is putting in place to assist recruitment and retention in the social care sector.
answer_text: My Department recognise that all levels of staff who work in social care are highly skilled, yet the remuneration is among the lowest in the labour market, leading to a low paid, undervalued and high turnover workforce, facing increasing pressures to provide the high quality of care the public demands.Staffing levels continue to be a significant challenge across the health and social care sector. My officials continue to work to maximise current capacity in social care and to engage with service providers in regard to addressing those ongoing issues of recruitment and retention.  On the 12th December 2024, my department launched the Social Care Workforce Strategy which sets out a vision to develop a sustainable social care workforce over the next 10 years.  The strategy is for those staff who are not required to hold a professional qualification to gain employment and who are working in statutory, private, community and voluntary settings across adult and children social care services.  The strategy includes actions to improve recruitment, retain social care staff and to raise the profile and recognition of the social care workforce as a skilled and competent workforce. In addition, my officials work in partnership with the Northern Ireland Social Care Council to develop the training, career and qualification pathways for the social care workforce which is key to supporting the recruitment and retention of social care practitioners. The Social Care Fair Work Forum has also been established which will seek to embed Fair Work initiatives and consider how we move the social care sector to a Real Living Wage employer.  Significant work is ongoing to build the case for the additional funding that will be required to deliver this. The Department does understand the concerns that these significant increases to National Insurance payments will have on providers in the independent social care sector. As with previous years, I would assure you that the Department is currently in the process of assessing the financial impact of these announcements and will be highlighting these inescapable pressures during the Budget process for 2025/26. It is planned to provide clarity as early as possible in the new year to ensure providers are able to plan effectively.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an updated timeline to meet requirements arising from the McCloud judgement.
answer_text: Under the Public Service Pensions and Judicial Offices Act 2022 my Department has direct responsibility for remedy requirements arising from the McCloud Judgment in respect of the Civil Service Pension Scheme. Following completion of the necessary secondary legislation and removal of the transitional protections which gave rise to the McCloud Judgment, the necessary processes are now in place to meet requirements for active members with service in the remedy period (1 April 2015 - 31 March 2022). Such members now receive a choice about their remedy benefit entitlements prior to retirement. Active members affected by the remedy have also received a personalised Remediable Service Statement (RSS) detailing their entitlements under the McCloud remedy. These members will continue to receive an updated statement of their entitlements annually until retirement.In respect of members with service in the remedy period who have already left service the requirement is that they are provided with a retrospective choice about their remedy service. This work is ongoing and my Department has started to issue choices to this cohort.TimelineThe legislation specifies that members should be provided with their retrospective choice by 31 March 2025 or a later date ‘as the scheme manager considers reasonable in all the circumstances in the case of a particular member or a particular class of member’.My Department is currently assessing what is achievable by 31 March 2025 and what is a realistic timeline for completion of this exercise. It should be noted this work involves complex and time-consuming personalised calculations based on members’ individual circumstances, and often complicated interactions with pensions taxation. As a consequence some members or particular classes of members may receive their retrospective choice after 31 March 2025. Communications are being developed to keep affected members updated.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on whether the deadline of the end of the 2024-25 financial year will be met for implementation of the McCloud judgement.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to answer provided for AQW 18748/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission what consideration has been given to increasing staff capacity following the Mediahuis judgement.
To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new build of Forge Integrated Primary School.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Carryduff greenway.
answer_text: Greenways are generally council led projects and Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council is leading on the development of the Carryduff greenway. I am keen to support the development of Greenways and have invited councils to submit applications for grant in support of Greenways and Active Travel enabling projects.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action he is taking to improve the quality of repair work on Housing Executive properties.

To ask the Minister of Health whether he will consider placing a guarantee of fair pay for health service workers into forthcoming Safe and Effective Staffing legislation, compelling pay rises to take place at the start of the financial year.
answer_text: The proposals set out in the Safe and Effective Staffing consultation outline plans to introduce a range of statutory duties relating to staffing matters. These include workforce planning, the use of common staffing methods and tools, and monitoring and reporting arrangements. I do not consider the proposed legislation to be the appropriate vehicle to set out pay uplifts and their timescales.We continue to operate on the basis of single year budgets, under which the Department’s financial outlook to be considered; it remains the case that opening budgets are insufficient to meet the cost of maintaining core services and meeting pay pressures. Discussions with the Executive around funding for any pay award and securing funding through monitoring rounds are major factors which determine when pay uplifts can take place. This means it would not be practicable to legally compel pay rises to take place at the start of the financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the action he is taking to ensure the arts sector in Northern Ireland has the funds to enable it to play a full part in UK arts exports.
answer_text: I continue to make the case for increased funding for this important sector.  It is my intention to provide a paper to the Executive showing the impact of the arts in our society to support that case.  I have also raised funding for the Arts directly in bilaterals and in writing with the Finance Minister.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how the priority projects currently being worked on by the Strategic Investment Board relate to the Programme for Government.
answer_text: SIB is engaged to support individual projects at the request of the relevant lead department, whose priorities will be informed by the Programme for Government. SIB attaches the highest importance to supporting departments to achieve the outcomes set out in the draft PfG, including major infrastructure and investment projects. SIB developed the draft Investment Strategy NI in parallel with TEO’s work on the Programme for Government and has ensured that it is consistent with the priorities of the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the action he is taking to address low pay in the arts sector.
answer_text: Funding for the arts sector is distributed on behalf of my Department by the Arts Council. It is not the role of my Department or the Arts Council to prescribe rates of pay.  The Arts Council is however committed to fair pay for artists and produces guidance on Rates of Pay for Artists which is accessible on its website at https://artscouncil-ni.s3-assets.com/ACNI-Rates-of-Pay-for-Artists-March-2022.pdf.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will engage with the Minister for Communities on how his Department's work on language, culture and heritage will dovetail with the appointment of the relevant commissioners under the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022.
answer_text: TEO is taking forward the necessary arrangements to implement the provisions of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (the Act) for which it is responsible, namely the establishment of the new bodies namely: The Office of Identity and Cultural Expression;The Irish Language Commissioner; andThe Commissioner for the Ulster Scots and the Ulster British tradition.The Department for Communities (DfC) remains policy lead for language policy including the development of the Ulster Scots, Language, Heritage and Culture strategy and the Irish Language strategy.A senior official from TEO has been in regular attendance at the DfC led cross departmental working group supporting the development of these Strategies.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the appointment of a Commissioner for Public Appointments.
answer_text: A public appointment competition was launched in 2021 for the role of Commissioner for Public Appointments for Northern Ireland, although an appointment decision was not reached prior to the end of the last mandate. We have agreed that a new competition is required. Preparations are currently underway to launch the appointment process.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the amount paid by their Department for (i) staff costs; (ii) office costs to cover the Northern Ireland Bureau in Beijing in financial year (a) 2021-22; (b) 2022-23; and (c) 2023-24.
answer_text: This budget, as requested, is set out below:(i) Staff Costs2023-20242022-20232021-2022Salary90,059.34223,962.99222,099.24Accommodation - Staff House Rent35,000.0035,000.0035,000.00Sub-total125,059.34258,962.99257,099.24(ii) Office Costs   Accommodation - Travel1,387.021,171.213,049.30Office Accommodation inc rates & charges58,867.0063,403.0058,967.15Travel & Subsistence1,919.903,627.394,717.80Sub-total62,173.9268,201.6066,734.25Total187,233.26327,164.59323,833.49

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 15015/22-27.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the steps they have taken to progress appointments to the boards of North/South bodies since 15 September 2024.
answer_text: The NSMC Joint Secretariat has asked those parties represented on the Executive to identify nominees to participate in a process to determine which board they will nominate too.It is intended that this process will be completed in the coming weeks, parties will then be asked to nominate individuals to those positions which are vacant.  Appointments can then be made at the next available NSMC meeting once nominees are identified.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any response he will be making to the think1265 Directed Time Budgeting and Workload Survey that demonstrates that rights under the workload agreement, which formed part of the pay deal with teachers before the current term began, are not being implemented.
answer_text: Teachers’ terms and conditions of service are negotiated through the forum known as the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) which comprises representatives of teachers’ employers, sectoral bodies, Department of Education and the five recognised teachers’ unions. The National Association of Schoolmasters Union of Women Teachers (NASUWT) is a member of TNC.Think 1265 is a website supported by the NASUWT but is not an NASUWT official website.Through TNC an agreed workload agreement (TNC 2011/8) was produced which explains the requirements of the 1265 directed hours of a full-time teacher. To support principals in the design and implementation of directed time budgets explanatory documentation including frequently asked questions and training has been jointly agreed with trade unions.The workload agreement has recently been reviewed through a joint working group which included a senior representative from NASUWT and a revised workload agreement was agreed and issued TNC 2024/2.The workload pressures on teachers are being considered through the recognised and accepted negotiating machinery of TNC.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the role the Education Authority has in ensuring workload agreements with teachers are implemented.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is represented on the Teachers' Negotiating Committee (TNC) which agreed and implemented the Workload Agreement TNC 2024/2. To ensure the workload agreement and directed time budgets (DTB) has been implemented the EA has provided the following support to schools:Updated online training for school Principals, Boards of Governors and Teachers covering directed time budgets and the Workload Agreement was devised and issued by the EA.A recording of the interactive training and guidance materials, including a walk-through of all resources and toolkits, enabling school leaders and Governors to access the DTB training at any time;The provision of locality 'DTB support and guidance' clinics for clusters and communities of school leaders as required;The provision of support and guidance for school leaders from their School Improvement Professional as required; andSchool Improvement Professionals supported individual schools to explore and engage with the featured modules within the recorded interactive training resource, which is hosted on The Supporting Learning website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any action being taken to ensure workload agreements with teachers are implemented as agreed during the current school term.
answer_text: The Teachers’ Workload Agreement, TNC 2024/2 has been agreed and was issued to all schools by the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) in April 2024. It recognises the importance of the role of the professional Teacher in ensuring that the educational needs of young people are met as fully as possible.It is the responsibility of Boards of Governors to ensure that the workload of all Principals, Vice Principals and Teachers is managed fairly and consistently to ensure that all teaching professionals can discharge their roles and responsibilities effectively for the benefit of the teaching and learning of pupils.The Department worked in conjunction with the Education Authority (EA) to update their online training for school Principals, Boards of Governors and Teachers covering directed time budgets and the Workload Agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health why the pay rise to health service workers, due in April 2024, has not yet been paid.
answer_text: Ahead of the NI Executive monitoring round, my Department was faced with a budget deficit in terms of available funding to meet the cost of maintaining core health and social care services at current levels.Despite an unprecedented level of savings of £200m implemented across HSC, the allocation of £350m in October monitoring, while welcome, has still left a funding gap of £100m to meet service pressures and pay uplifts.Last week I met with Trade Unions representing Agenda for Change health staff and firm proposals on a pay deal have been put to them, which they will now consider. It is important to recognise that further work still needs to be undertaken in order to reach a resolution to the issue of health service pay. It is also important to recognise that the challenges facing public sector pay in general will only be resolved through an all-executive approach.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his Department's assessment of how the workload agreement, which was part of the pay deal agreed with teachers after he came to office, is currently functioning.
answer_text: The teachers' Workload Agreement TNC 2024/2 was jointly developed and agreed by both the teaching trade unions (NITC) and Management Side of TNC.The central purpose of the agreement is to enable Principals, Vice Principals and Teachers to achieve the following objectives:a. to maintain and enhance the quality of teaching and learning; b. to preserve, in so far as this is possible, the flexibility of the contractual framework and the professional autonomy of staff; c. to ensure that members of staff are able to observe their contractual requirements, pursue their careers and undertake their day-to-day duties without unfair and unreasonable requirements being placed on them in relation to those duties and with sufficient time to enjoy a reasonable work/life balance; d. to protect and enhance the professional relationships within a school; e. to ensure that Boards of Governors as employers are aware of and in a position to meet their legal obligations with respect to employment matters and to operate within the spirit of the legislation governing Working Time and Health and Safety.The agreement clarifies the legislative terms and conditions of teaching staff including working hours, teaching time, cover arrangements and supervision.The guidance outlined in the agreement has been developed to provide a framework for Principals and Boards of Governors to ensure the allocation and distribution of duties to all Principals, Vice Principals and Teachers is managed in a fair and reasonable manner that enables staff to observe their contractual requirements with sufficient time to enjoy a reasonable work/life balance.I am satisfied that the Workload Agreement TNC 2024/2 is effective in providing clarification, advice and guidance on teachers' terms and conditions to Boards of Governors, Principals and Teachers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his Department's assessment of the accessibility of the proposed site for the new build of Forge Integrated Primary school; and (ii) when the outcome of this assessment will be communicated to the Board of Governors and Principal of the school.
answer_text: I can advise that my department has been consulted on a Pre-Application Discussion (PAD) with proposals to develop the site of the former Knockbreda High School.  My officials have given full consideration to all the options presented, including any potential effect on road safety and traffic progression, and have provided without prejudice, advice and recommendations to the planning authority.  My department has engaged positively with the Department for Education during the PAD process and welcomes the development of the site.At this time, the Department has not been consulted upon an any further proposals through the Planning Authority. However, officials will continue to assess any design proposals and provide advice and recommendations on road safety and traffic progression, as required, as part of the consultation process. The planning authority has advised that a planning application has yet to be lodged to Belfast City Council in respect of the proposals for a new build of Forge Integrated Primary School.  I can also advise that my officials responded to correspondence from the Principal of Forge Integrated Primary School on 11th December 2023, outlining my Department’s position in relation to the proposed development of the former Knockbreda High School site.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how much funding his Department will allocate for barriers at park and ride facilities, such as Cairnshill Park and Ride, to ensure only passengers of park and ride facilities can avail of free parking.
answer_text: Officials are currently considering the provision of a number of measures for Cairnshill and Black’s Road Park & Rides sites. Until these measures have been agreed and finalised, officials are not in a position to advise on the associated costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide the documentation outlining the evidence that the A55 Knock dual carriageway is a protected route.
answer_text: In May 2023, my Department formally adopted The Belfast Local Development Plan 2035 (LDP), Plan Strategy 2035 and this Strategy has become the principal consideration when determining planning applications for development in Belfast.Policy TRAN 7 “Access to protected routes”, within the Strategy sets out the planning policy in relation to access to protected routes in the Belfast City Council area.  https://www.belfastcity.gov.uk/getmedia/bba4bd89-157d-4209-8d5e-8a6eb58dd760/PS001-Plan-Strategy-Final-Web-May2023.pdfMy Department has published a Protected Routes map identifying roads throughout Northern Ireland which have been established as Protected Routes and their associated classifications.  The A55 is classified as “Dual carriageway, Ring Road, Through-Pass and By-Pass” in some locations with others classified in the category “Other Protected Routes”https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/development-control-information-developers

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline what proposals DfI Roads have put forward to enable road access to the site of the new build of Forge Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: I can advise that my department has been consulted on a Pre-Application Discussion (PAD) with proposals to develop the site of the former Knockbreda High School.  My officials have given full consideration to all the options presented, including any potential effect on road safety and traffic progression, and have provided without prejudice, advice and recommendations to the planning authority.  My department has engaged positively with the Department for Education during the PAD process and welcomes the development of the site.At this time, the Department has not been consulted upon an any further proposals through the Planning Authority. However, officials will continue to assess any design proposals and provide advice and recommendations on road safety and traffic progression, as required, as part of the consultation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the pre-application discussion regarding road access to the new build of Forge Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: I can advise that my department has been consulted on a Pre-Application Discussion (PAD) with proposals to develop the site of the former Knockbreda High School.  My officials have given full consideration to all the options presented, including any potential effect on road safety and traffic progression, and have provided without prejudice, advice and recommendations to the planning authority.  My department has engaged positively with the Department for Education during the PAD process and welcomes the development of the site.At this time, the Department has not been consulted upon an any further proposals through the Planning Authority. However, officials will continue to assess any design proposals and provide advice and recommendations on road safety and traffic progression, as required, as part of the consultation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how the current Physician Associate Masters course, with a significant number of those qualified unable to find work in the health service, fits within the overall workforce planning policy.
answer_text: My Department leads long-term, regional workforce planning.  The focus is strategic, including all of the HSC and the independent, voluntary, community and private sectors.  The aim is to secure workforce supply across the entire HSC over a 5 – 10-year horizon.  It is aligned to, and an enabler of, the Transformation agenda.While concentrating on workforce supply, it also considers opportunities for workforce development.  The expansion of multi-disciplinary working is a key part of this, with potential to increase the efficiency and effectiveness of the health and social care workforce.  As qualified healthcare professionals, Physician Associates can make an important contribution within that workforce. Action 2.18 of my Department’s Workforce Strategy – “Planning for and introducing new roles” – includes an objective to develop a NI-wide strategy for the utilisation of Physician Associates across the HSC.  My Department’s officials therefore continue to encourage employers across the HSC Trusts to secure posts for the graduates from the MSc in Physician Associate Studies degree programme at Ulster University, despite the very challenging financial situation faced by the HSC.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether there are plans to enhance the role of the Mater Hospital as part of the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust's maternity services.
answer_text: Professor Mary Renfrew’s report entitled 'Enabling Safe Quality Midwifery Services and Care in Northern Ireland' was published on 22 October 2024.The report has outlined the need for alternatives to hospital-based care.  Professor Renfrew further states that at least one community midwifery hub should be available in all HSC Trusts and that the sites of the free-standing midwifery-led units (which includes the Mater Hospital) should be developed and reconfigured as community midwifery hubs in a phased programme to ensure safe, quality care for all.  The Department of Health will be setting up an oversight board to consider the recommendations outlined in the report.The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust’s Midwifery team will work under the strategic direction of the oversight board going forward in order to deliver against any of the agreed actions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the new cohort of Physician Associate Masters students, commencing study in January 2025, will be reasonably guaranteed positions within the health service at the end of their studies.
answer_text: My Department’s officials will continue to work proactively with employers across the HSC Trusts in trying to secure posts for future graduates from the MSc in Physician Associate Studies degree programme at Ulster University.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline the current cost of providing 20 two-year Masters courses for Physician Associates.
answer_text: My Department currently incurs costs of £787k for a cohort of 20 places on the two-year MSc in Physician Associates Studies at Ulster University. My Department pays students’ tuition fees and provides funding to HSC Trusts and General Practitioners to facilitate clinical placements in accordance with curriculum requirements.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health why home births are not signposted as part of the information on location of maternity services in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust website.
answer_text: Belfast Health and Social Care Trust have advised that in order to access Maternity and Neonatal website references to Home Birth - use search option at top of website page (enter your search here) - key word - Home Birth.1) Under Labour and Birth- see link https://belfasttrust.hscni.net/services/maternity/pregnancy-journey/labour-and-birth/ - scroll down to Home Birth heading.2) Under CoMC (Continuity of Carer) - see link https://belfasttrust.hscni.net/services/maternity/pregnancy-journey/continuity-of-midwifery-carer/  scroll down to 3rd paragraph - 'The Continuity of Midwifery Carer Team provide 24/7 availability for labour and birth and support women in all birth choice settings including home birth provision'.3) Care Opinion - Alice's Story - feedback February - https://belfasttrust.hscni.net/contact-us/compliments-and-complaints/care-opinion/care-opinion-4/ scroll down under the ' The Continuity of Midwifery Carer service' 3rd paragraph4) Community Midwifery Service - see 'Their role' includes 2nd bullet point - https://belfasttrust.hscni.net/service/community-midwife-service/5) RJMS - Hospital Recruitment - Why we work in Belfast - https://belfasttrust.hscni.net/working-for-us/where-you-could-work/new-royal-jubilee-maternity-services-recruitment-campaign/

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of what investment is required to ensure the good jobs employment rights Bill addresses any negative impact that a lack of adequate provision for people with learning disabilities has on the economy.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on research commissioned by the Executive Office into Historical Clerical Child Abuse.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of delays to the North – South Belfast Glider Route, to detail any additional public transport provision he is planning on the Belfast, Cairnshill and Saintfield corridor over the remainder of the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and can confirm that Translink continually review service provision across the network to ensure it meets the needs of the public. However, at this time, Translink have no immediate plans to make significant changes to the public transport services on the Belfast, Cairnshill, and Saintfield corridor. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how long the current closure of Durham Street in Belfast city centre will impact on journey times for those who need to use their car during rush hour, for example to collect their children from different locations.
answer_text: I can advise that the current closure of Durham Street is programmed to last for 12 months and finish on the 12 October 2025.  The closure is required to facilitate the provision of public realm works around the new Belfast Grand Central Station and the associated removal of the bridge. I appreciate that this work is causing additional congestion to the road network and I acknowledge that traffic congestion in Belfast has been a significant concern for road users over the past number of months.  I would encourage travellers to avoid traffic congestion by considering other sustainable and active modes of travel where possible such as public transport, using the park and ride sites or by walking or cycling.  Encouraging use of alternative modes to access the city centre will help alleviate some congestion for those who essentially need to travel by private car.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the work of the Department on an Irish Language Strategy.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any measures he is putting in place to assist those who need to use their car in Belfast city centre, for example to collect their children from different locations, but are impacted by the current closure of Durham Street.
answer_text: I acknowledge that traffic congestion in Belfast has been a significant concern for road users over the past number of months.  There have been a number of factors which have contributed to this traffic congestion, including planned and emergency roadworks and also the impact and management of incidents and traffic collisions across the road network.The roadworks that are ongoing throughout Belfast and elsewhere characterise change.  The essential upgrading of our road, water, transportation and energy infrastructure is critical to the modernisation and progression of our economy, health, and education sectors, as are private industrial, commercial, retail and residential developments.  On completion, these facilities often provide vital services to our citizens.Roadworks are carefully planned and programmed to minimise their impact on road users as much as possible.  However, given the scale and complexity of certain projects, some overlap is unavoidable and some congestion is inevitable.Whilst I understand that traffic delays are frustrating, I would encourage travellers to avoid traffic congestion by considering other sustainable and active modes of travel where possible such as public transport, using the park and ride sites or by walking or cycling.  Encouraging use of alternative modes to access the city centre will help alleviate some congestion for those who essentially need to travel by private car.You will also be aware of my recent announcement to pause non-essential roadworks on key routes into Belfast in the run up to Christmas, commencing on Monday 25th November.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what action is she taking to contribute toward ending violence against women and girls.

To ask the Minister of Education whether he is bringing forward any proposals to enhance the partnership between schools and local councils to maintain and expand community sport facilities, particularly in line with research showing the benefits of participation in sport on mental health.
answer_text: My officials have been engaging regularly with local councils and schools in recent months on the RAISE programme to explore potential joint working across education and local government. We are keen to collaborate with stakeholders across Northern Ireland to support children’s learning and development, including their mental health.  The RAISE programme provides an opportunity to embed such collaboration on a range of issues.Schools are essential in encouraging healthy living, nurturing positive perceptions of physical activity, and enhancing awareness of its beneficial effects on health and well-being, as well as promoting sportsmanship and fair play. It is recommended by my department that schools offer students a minimum of two hours of high-quality curricular PE each week.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of visits he has undertaken with youth services delivered by the community and voluntary sector since coming to office.
answer_text: Since coming to office I have not visited any statutory youth services, however I have visited four youth service settings delivered by the community and voluntary sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of visits he has undertaken with statutory youth services since coming to office.
answer_text: Since coming to office I have not visited any statutory youth services, however I have visited four youth service settings delivered by the community and voluntary sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the plans for developing the roles and functions of statutory youth services.
answer_text: There are no current plans to develop the role and function of statutory youth services.  A review of the Priorities for Youth policy is currently in progress and this will inform further developments within both the statutory and voluntary youth sectors.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, given ongoing traffic issues in the area, whether any further work has been carried out to check that the traffic light phasing at Finaghy crossroads is optimal.
answer_text: The Finaghy crossroads is an extremely busy junction with 10 vehicle and pedestrian movements to be managed safely and efficiently.To achieve this, my Department introduced a state-of-the-art form of dynamic traffic signal control called MOVA (Microprocessor Optimised Vehicle Actuation).  This measures traffic flow on each approach and adapts signal timings, in real time, according to prevailing traffic flow, to ensure optimal traffic flow at this junction.The junction is also actively monitored using the Department’s CCTV camera network and signal timings are adjusted to reflect traffic conditions and to reduce congestion. As such, I can confirm that the traffic light phasing is considered to be optimal.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has any plans of improving compliance in relation to not parking in cycle lanes.
answer_text: The Traffic Management (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 gives my Department the power to issue Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) when a vehicle is detected parked on a legislated Mandatory Cycle Lane.Going forward, my Department will also be putting in place legislation for segregated cycle tracks to ensure that parking restrictions can be enforced.  Vehicles causing obstruction by parking on Advisory Cycle Lanes or Footway Cycle Tracks, where no parking restrictions are marked on the carriageway, are a matter for the PSNI.My Department continues to work with various stakeholders to encourage parking compliance across the road network and would urge all drivers to 'think before they park’, to park responsibly and legally, and to consider the potential negative impact of their actions on all other road users.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the lane arrangements at Finaghy crossroads, given that traffic, notably going in the direction from Finaghy Road South to Finaghy Road North, is often unable to move during peak times as it is obstructed by vehicles turning left.
answer_text: I can advise that the current traffic signal sequencing at Finaghy crossroads is complex, with up to 6 traffic movements and 4 pedestrian movements that need to be controlled efficiently but more importantly, safely, within the limited road space available.During peak periods the traffic lanes leading to and from this junction run at full capacity and with limited road space there is only one traffic lane on the legs exiting the junction. Due to the bus stop on the Upper Lisburn Road country bound side of the junction, traffic on occasions tails back to the lights causing congestion.Given the limited road space, the current traffic signalling arrangement and road marking layout at this junction provides the best possible balance between the competing demands of through and turning traffic. There are no plans to make any changes at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any work is being undertaken to improve compliance with urban clearways on major thoroughfares, such as the A1 Lisburn Road.
answer_text: Parking enforcement patrols are designed to focus enforcement in areas of most need to reduce traffic congestion, improve bus journey times and road safety and provide accessibility for all road and pavement users, including Blue Badge holders.Enforcement patrols are specifically developed for each area, including the A1 Lisburn Road, and are kept under review to ensure they remain proportionate and address the needs of the area. My officials are satisfied that the current level of enforcement on Urban Clearways, such as the A1 Lisburn Road, reflects the needs of the area.Nonetheless, my Department’s parking enforcement approach is flexible and, resource permitting, can be adjusted accordingly. Requests for additional enforcement can be made to the following email address Parking.Request@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on work to improve mutual recognition of training between the Irish Medical Council and General Medical Council.
answer_text: The General Medical Council (GMC) continues to recognise for full registration the qualifications of doctors in the Republic of Ireland provided they are contained on its list of acceptable GP and specialist qualifications.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, given the urgency of transformation measures, to detail the rationale for the consultation period for the Hospitals - Creating a Better Network for Outcomes running from 1 October 2024 to 21 January 2025.
answer_text: My Department is committed to having an honest dialogue and better communication with the public, one that creates opportunities for people who use our services to have a say in how they are planned and delivered. I believe that allowing the public this period of time to respond to such an important document as Hospitals – Creating a Network for Better Outcomes is appropriate, especially since this will run over the busy Christmas period. Furthermore, I do not believe that this will be to the detriment of my reform agenda. Reform initiatives will continue to be progressed whilst the consultation is live. This includes, for example, my Live Better initiative to address health inequalities. Our Trusts will also continue advance hospital reconfiguration processes, if necessary, such as in the case of general surgery in the Northern Health and Social Care Trust, which is currently out for public consultation. I would encourage all those with an interest in the hospital network document to respond to the consultation, which is now live

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any work undertaken to implement the recommendations of the Nuffield Trust September 2022 report, Future Funding and Productivity in Northern Ireland's Health and Social Care System.
answer_text: The report by the Nuffield Trust in September 2022 outlined a number of findings on efficiency and productivity across Northern Ireland's Health and Social Care System (HSC). The updated Elective Care Framework (ECF), published in May 2024, sets out the strategic direction for elective care with targeted actions aimed at improving productivity and efficiency and driving value for money. Against a challenging financial backdrop, a programme of work is well underway to ensure optimal efficiency and throughput across the HSC system.  The aim is to maximise the resources at our disposal to ultimately deliver greater levels of activity and access to high quality elective care services on a sustainable basis for patients waiting for treatment.  I will continue to drive through the improvements needed to ensure the effectiveness and efficiency of services, wherever and whenever possible.
