question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the timeline for mutual recognition of training on a cross-border basis to ensure doctors from Northern Ireland are able to apply for the full range of consultant posts, notably in paediatric cardiology.
answer_text: The General Medical Council (GMC) continues to recognise the qualifications of doctors who qualified for full registration in the Republic of Ireland provided they are continued on its list of acceptable GP and specialist qualifications. The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) Paediatric Cardiology speciality qualification is not on this list. However, on 15 May 2024, the GMC launched its Recognised Specialist Qualification (RSQ) pathway which provides a new route for assurance as to a doctor’s knowledge, skills and experience for registration. RCPI are currently engaging with the GMC to understand the requirements for their specialty qualification to be included in the GMC’s list.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps he is taking, including a timescale, to (i) speed up the removal of posters expressing racial hatred placed on departmental property; and (ii) improve engagement with the PSNI on this issue.
answer_text: Racism is a scourge in our society, and there can be no tolerance for hatred and discrimination in our communities.The display of a poster expressing racial hatred is a criminal offence. When a crime has been committed, it is for the police to investigate and to act to resolve matters and I would encourage anyone with information in regards the display of such items to contact the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has guidelines requiring midwives to have a driving licence upon appointment.
answer_text: An effective midwifery service is vitally important to delivering safe, quality and equitable care to women, babies and families. My Department is commited to improving health and social care outcomes for the population of Northern Ireland.  This is underpinned by the Chief Nursing Officer’s ‘Shaping Our Future’ vision, launched in May last year, which focuses on sustainability, recruitment, retention and the value of both midwives and nurses.My Department does not employ practising midwives.  I am advised by HSC Trusts, as employers, that driving licences are necessary for rotational or community midwives.  Any post-specific guidelines or requirements of employment would be stated in recruitment advertisements and job specifications issued by Trusts.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when rail services will commence from Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: The Department is responsible for ensuring NI Railways’ regulatory compliance with the rail safety and interoperability regulations and grants authority for new infrastructure to enter into service. Officials are working through the process with the safety of the travelling passengers paramount. Translink have now announced that rail services will commence from 13th October..

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of recent developments on City Deal funding, for an update on the North Belfast to South Belfast Glider route proposal.
answer_text: Funding for the Belfast Region City Deal has been confirmed by the UK Treasury and the deal is no longer paused.£35m for the delivery of the North – South Belfast Glider Route is identified with in this Deal.  The estimated total scheme cost is in the range £142 to £148m, and therefore a significant shortfall of some £110m remains.  I am committed to working with Executive colleagues and City Deal partners to ensure that the appropriate level of investment is made available to deliver this project.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any assistance his Department provides to midwives who do not have a driving licence.
answer_text: I refer you to my answer to AQW 15536/22-27, which confirms that rotational or community midwifery roles do require a full driving licence.  I note that the three current recruitment adverts for midwifery (within SEHSCT, WHSCT and the NHSCT) confirm this position, but also that the requirement can be waived for any applicant whose disability prohibits driving but has access to a form of transport approved by the Trust, which permits them to carry out the duties of the post.  Any post-specific flexibilities or exceptions will be stated in recruitment advertisements and job specifications issued by Trusts.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current target date for the provision of a 24/7 thrombectomy service.
answer_text: The expansion of the regional thrombectomy service to 24/7 remains a key priority in Northern Ireland.  This will require significant investment including the recruitment of specialist posts within a limited workforce pool. Given the budget, which you supported, I am not in a position to give a target date. However, I am committed to ensuring as many people as possible from across Northern Ireland continue to benefit from the service within the current hours of operation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the nature of the clear and unequivocal advice that enabled him, alongside the First Minister and deputy First Minister, to support an extension on the ban on puberty blockers, including (i) the source of the advice; and (iii) whether the advice will be published.
answer_text: A UK-Wide Statutory Instrument entitled “The Medicines (Gonadotrophin-Releasing Hormone Analogues) (Emergency Prohibition) (Extension) Order 2024” was made and laid in UK parliament on 22 August 2024 to extend a temporary ban recently on the supply of puberty blockers on a UK-wide basis to include Northern Ireland following the findings of a recent independent review of gender identity services for children and young people in England (“the Cass Review”) and in light of subsequent advice received from Senior Professional advisers from within my Department.The findings of the Cass Review, which were published on 10 April 2024, found that there is not currently a reliable evidence-base upon which to make clinical decisions about the use of “puberty blockers” to treat gender dysphoria/incongruence, or for children and their families to make informed choices. It also concluded that because of the limited evidence, and potential risks to patient safety with regards neurocognitive development, psychosexual development and longer-term bone health, these medicines should only be offered for this purpose under a research protocol.To this end the current UK-wide temporary emergency prohibition order was made under powers conferred by Section 62 of the Medicines Act 1968 (“the Act”) which enables Ministers to prohibit the sale, supply or importation of medicines on safety grounds. Section 62(3) of the Act specifically allows for the making of an emergency order without formal consultation when in the opinion of Ministers it is essential to make the order with immediate effect to avoid serious danger to health.  The First Minister and deputy First Minister agreed with my recommendation based on the evidence that the emergency prohibition order should extend to Northern Ireland. The safety of patients is paramount, and my Department will continue to work with the Department of Health and Social Care in England and the other UK administrations to consider the development and implementation of a permanent solution to this issue which will require consultation with representative organisations in Northern Ireland.  Any permanent proposals arising from consultation will of course be brought to the Executive for its agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill, as currently planned, will address gaps in holiday pay and sick leave for substitute teachers.
answer_text: Terms and conditions of service for workers in specific public services are a matter for the employing authorities and for the relevant Departments.The Good Jobs Employment Bill Consultation, which is ongoing, seeks views on the proposal to extend the holiday pay calculation reference period for workers on variable hours from 12 weeks to 52 weeks.I encourage everyone, including those who have sector specific concerns about how any proposals may affect them, or their industry, to respond to the consultation.The Department for Communities is responsible for statutory sick pay.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any discussions he has had with the Minister for the Economy in relation to the inclusion of substitute teachers, and other substitute staff working in schools, in the forthcoming Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: The Minister has asked his officials to consider with the employers the implications of the Bill in relation to supply teachers and any other groups of temporary staff.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any work he is undertaking to enhance understanding of Ulster-Scots culture and heritage in schools, including through visits to the Ulster-Scots Hub in Belfast city centre.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland curriculum provides significant scope for the teaching of the Ulster-Scots language, literature, culture and history at both primary and post-primary level. These can be delivered through areas such as Language & Literacy; Environment & Society; and Learning for Life and Work / Local and Global Citizenship, where young people are able to explore their own and others’ cultural identity, heritage and diversity.There are also specific units within A-level and GCSE History which enable students to learn about the origins of Ulster-Scots, the actions of Ulster-Scots in the 18th century and the contributions of Ulster-Scots men and women in the 19th and early 20th centuries.The Department of Education delegates as much funding and decision-making as possible to schools to decide which resources or programmes they use to support delivery of the curriculum.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any discussions he has had with the Minister for the Economy in relation to the impact of the forthcoming Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill on (i) the teaching profession in general; and (ii) substitute teachers.
answer_text: The Minister has asked his officials to consider with the employers of teachers, including supply teachers, the implications of the Bill in relation to supply teachers and any other groups of temporary staff.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on (i) the competition launched by the Strategic Investment Board with a closing date of 16 February 2024 to recruit a Technical Director for the Casement Park project; and (ii) whether this role had been filled prior to 13 September 2024.
answer_text: The competition concluded without the appointment of a Technical Director therefore the role was not filled.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on work to provide and promote a GCSE course in Sign Language.
answer_text: I refer the member to my previous answer to AQW 13803/22-27, answered on 28 June 2024.  A process is in train to develop a GCSE in British Sign Language in England.  Once that qualification is available in England it can be accredited for use in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in relation to the Bengoa principle of prevention and early intervention, when the age for bowel cancer screening in Northern Ireland will be reduced from the current age of 60, in line with the rest of the UK.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any plans they have to enhance their relationship with North-South Bodies, including through more regular meetings with Directors and Chief Executives.
answer_text: Executive Ministers and Junior Ministers regularly meet representatives of the North South Bodies, both at NSMC meetings and other events, and will continue to do so.The North South Bodies deliver important public services, and we received an update on their work at the recent NSMC Plenary held on 13 September.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any action they have taken to implement Recommendation 1 of the Covid-19 Inquiry, in relation to the simplification of structures dealing with civil emergencies and resilience.
answer_text: TEO officials are working to understand how the recommendations contained in the Covid 19 Inquiry Module 1 Report can be best delivered within the scope of the Northern Ireland Civil Contingencies Framework and the Civil Contingencies Act 2004.A cross-departmental steering group is due to meet in the near future to consider and progress implementation of the recommendations from the Covid 19 Inquiry Module 1 Report.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update to Paragraph 2.80 of the Covid-19 Inquiry, in relation to a gap in the role of Chief Scientific Adviser to the Executive as a whole.
answer_text: Professor Helen McCarthy was appointed as the Northern Ireland Executive’s first Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser (CSTA) in June 2024.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any actions they will be taking during September 2024 to fill the vacancies on the Board of the North-South Language Board.
answer_text: We have asked the NSMC Joint Secretariat to engage with those parties represented on the Executive to identify nominees for board members of all North South Bodies, including the North South Language Body.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any guidance they have issued to Departments on meeting the requirements of Recommendation 15 in Chapter 10 of the Hate Crime Review, concerning the removal of expressions of hate.
answer_text: The Executive Office has not issued guidance regarding Recommendation 15 in Chapter 10 of the Hate Crime Review.The Department of Justice is progressing policy development in relation to the Hate Crime Review and has indicated that recommendation 15 will be included in phase two of the public consultation later in the current mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his Department's plans to enhance tourism links with the United States of America ahead of the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence in 2026.
answer_text: Tourism Ireland is responsible for promoting the island of Ireland overseas as a holiday and Business Events destination and undertakes marketing campaigns in key source markets for tourism. They have 16 offices overseas and have two office in the United States, New York and San Francisco.Two Tourism Ireland staff members are members of the United States/Northern Ireland Cultural Working Group that was set up this summer. Tourism Ireland’s Head of North America made a presentation at a meeting this summer, outlining potential tourism opportunities to leverage the 250th anniversary of the signing of the US Declaration of Independence. Future meetings will be attended by Tourism Ireland.Tourism Ireland’s Chief Executive and Head of Stakeholder Engagement recently visited Hillsborough Castle, which has a particular connection with, and stories to tell about, the United States’ moment of independence. Tourism Ireland will leverage these connections and other opportunities to celebrate the anniversary.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the steps he is taking to meet Recommendation 15 in Chapter 10 of the Hate Crime Review, issued by Judge Desmond Marrinan, concerning the removal of expressions of hate.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive recognises the removal of hate expressions and addressing hate crimes in Northern Ireland requires a concerted and collaborative effort across government, civil society, and communities. By adopting a comprehensive strategy that prioritises dialogue, education, and awareness, Northern Ireland can move towards a more inclusive society that honours its diverse identities and fosters peaceful coexistence.The Housing Executive are acutely aware that this is a very emotive area which requires a sensitive approach, and the Housing Executive will continue to work towards the removal of hate expressions and addressing hate crime which is intrinsic to its everyday work in communities.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on how community sector organisations will be supported in preparing to celebrate the links between Northern Ireland and the United States ahead of the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence in 2026.
answer_text: The 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence presents a unique opportunity to promote the cultural and historical links between Northern Ireland and the United States and the significant role that people from here played in shaping America. I established the United States / Northern Ireland Cultural Working Group to explore new opportunities and to develop a programme of cultural engagement to further the historical links that exist.Community sector organisations that want to mark the anniversary here will be able to look towards the wide range of existing funding schemes that support community activity, such as the Community Festivals Fund which my department supports in partnership with the local councils. The normal application and governance arrangements for such schemes are likely to continue to apply. National Museums Northern Ireland will be preparing a programme of events and activities focussed at the Ulster American Folk Park. While Libraries NI do not at present have any specific plans, they will continue to consider the potential for public libraries to support the marking of the anniversary.There is huge potential to exploit all the links and I am keen to see everybody involved in making sure that we make the most of this unique opportunity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on work undertaken by Tourism NI in relation to enhancing inward tourism from the United States of America, particularly with regard to the Ulster-Scots and Scotch-Irish heritage.
answer_text: It is estimated there are twenty to fifty million people in the US of Ulster-Scots descent.  Tourism Ireland’s “US Market - A Strategy for Growth 2018-2025” sets out the ambition for the US market together with a strategy and action plan to drive growth, owned collectively by Tourism Ireland, Fáilte Ireland, Tourism NI and industry. The strategy sets out an ambitious agenda to grow visitor numbers and revenue on the island of Ireland and one of its objectives is to leverage niche products and experiences including the Irish Diaspora, and Scots Irish / Ulster Scots.   A US/North of Ireland Working Group has been established that includes Tourism NI, Tourism Ireland, the Ulster-Scots Agency and other stakeholders to reflect on the shared cultural heritage.  A programme of events is being planned to promote and celebrate the Ulster-Scots contribution to the creation and development of the US in the run up to the 250th celebrations. To drive wider business from the US market, Tourism NI delivers an annual programme of activity aimed at increasing programming and inbound bookings from international tour operators working in the sectors of travel trade, golf and business tourism. Tourism NI support the North’s tourism industry to attend major trade shows in the US such as IMEX America which is the largest meeting of event planners in the world, and last year welcomed 76 buyers from the US market to its annual flagship event “Meet the Buyer”.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004 will be updated to include the term Universal Credit.
answer_text: Work is underway in advance of updating the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004. The focus of this work is to reinstate automatic passporting for those eligible to receive free sight tests and free Health Service dental treatment through the Help with Health Care (HwHC) scheme following the migration of their benefits to Universal Credit (UC) by the Department for Communities (DfC). Currently there is no automatic entitlement system for UC recipients. As an interim solution, UC recipients must annually apply through the Department’s Low Income Scheme. These individuals must complete a paper based application form called a HC1 and return it to DfC who will issue a health service certificate to those who are eligible. This has obvious implications for some of our most vulnerable people and it is clear that there is an urgent need to update our legislation to reinstate automatic passporting to those who are eligible. It is not the case that in Great Britain (GB) all Universal Credit recipients are automatically eligible for help with health care costs including free eye tests. In GB, an earnings threshold is applied alongside Universal Credit to determine eligibility The Department of Health is currently liaising with other Northern Ireland departments and colleagues in GB. This will inform the decision on setting the income threshold for UC becoming an automated passport benefit. A short public consultation will be conducted in the coming months on proposed changes to our legislation in relation to UC, with the associated legislative process commencing in due course thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any steps he is taking to meet Recommendation 15 in Chapter 10 of the Hate Crime Review, issued by Judge Desmond Marrinan, concerning the removal of expressions of hate.
answer_text: The issue of unauthorised flags on Departmental property is a sensitive one and has been ongoing for many years. My Department’s primary consideration is the safety of road users and where unauthorised flags or attachments pose a risk to road users, my Department will seek to remove that hazard. Where there is no such danger, my Department will liaise closely with other key stakeholders and seek to provide a solution.Finding a sustainable solution to the ongoing problem of illegally erected flags and emblems requires leadership and a united approach. Until such times as a comprehensive approach can be developed, my Department will continue to apply its current processes. I will continue to engage with my Executive colleagues on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the new vaccination for respiratory syncytial virus will be made available for (i) older people aged 75 to 79; and (ii) women who are 28 weeks pregnant or more.
answer_text: The Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) vaccination programmes commenced in Northern Ireland from the 1st September 2024.The RSV programme for older adults will be administered mainly by GPs, while HSC Trust Vaccination Teams will offer vaccination to eligible individuals in care homes or those who are housebound. The RSV vaccination programme for pregnant women will be administered by HSC Trusts.  This will be led by Trust vaccination services in collaboration with Trust maternity services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any steps he is taking to amend the restrictions on drivers within one year of passing their test, including reference to the 45mph speed limit applying on motorways and high-quality dual carriageways where other cars are subject to a 70mph speed limit.
answer_text: The Road Traffic (Amendment) Act 2016 provides the statutory framework for graduated driver licensing. This includes provision for the removal of the 45mph speed restriction on learner and newly qualified drivers and for learning and driving on motorways.Further subordinate legislation, dedicated funding and significant operational planning activity are required in advance of implementation. I have asked my officials to seek legal advice which will help me determine the best way forward. At this stage however, I am unable to provide a timeline for GDL implementation.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when amendments to the Ministerial Code, expected within 100 days of the restoration of the Assembly, will be made.

To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for his assessment of the requirement that guarantors for rental tenants must be resident in Northern Ireland; and (ii) to detail the guidance his Department offers on this matter.
answer_text: Private tenancies legislation does not include any stipulations in relation to requirements for guarantors, and it is a matter for landlords/agents as to whether they wish to ask for this form of security.  Landlords/agents also take alternative forms of security, such as, rent in advance and/or references from previous landlords.   	My Department does not have any guidance on this matter.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will sign off on the Executive's Climate Action Plan.
answer_text: Development of the Executive’s Climate Action Plan is being led by the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs.As a significant piece of cross-cutting work it is expected that the Climate Action Plan will likely require approval of the Executive Committee.To date, the draft Climate Action Plan has not been shared with us for consideration or sign off, along with other Ministerial colleagues.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the appointment of the Chair and Board of the Victims and Survivors Service, which was expected by May 2024.
answer_text: Following a public appointment process, we have agreed the successful candidate for the post of Chair and three successful candidates for Board member posts. An announcement will be made once all the relevant paperwork is processed, and the necessary checks carried out.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the appointment of Board members to North-South Implementation Bodies.
answer_text: We refer the member to the answer provided on 20 June in relation to AQW 11629/22-27.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Climate Commissioner regulations.
answer_text: Development of the Regulations required under Section 50 of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 is progressing well. These Regulations will be a first step in establishing the NI Climate Commissioner.We will consider the draft Regulations in the weeks ahead at which point next steps can be determined.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the appointment of a Climate Commissioner, which was expected by May 2024.
answer_text: We have agreed that the necessary procedures should be undertaken to have the regulations to establish a Climate Commissioner here laid and made in this Assembly as soon as possible. The appointment of the Climate Commissioner will follow once the Assembly procedures are completed and the regulations are in place.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Strategic Framework for Ending Violence against Women and Girls, which was expected by May 2024.
answer_text: Ending Violence Against Women and Girls remains one of our key priorities. We are currently considering final options and the updated Framework and Action Plan will be brought to the Executive at the earliest opportunity.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the review of Together: Building a United Community, which was expected by May 2024.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Refugee Integration Strategy, which was expected by May 2024.
answer_text: We are currently considering a revised draft Refugee Integration Strategy and associated Thematic Delivery Plan. We intend to seek Executive agreement in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Investment Strategy for Northern Ireland, which was expected by summer 2024.
answer_text: We are considering a draft of the Investment Strategy. We hope to be able to bring it to the Executive for approval in the near future.  Approval of the Investment Strategy will then be a matter for the Executive.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Historical Institutional Abuse Redress Review, which was expected by summer 2024.
answer_text: Since receiving the Review report, our officials have engaged with victims and survivors on its findings and have worked with key partners in the HIA Redress Board, COSICA and VSS to implement operational / administrative recommendations, to improve the process for victims and survivors.  This has included additional resource for VSS, a public awareness campaign and a new Code of Practice for solicitors developed with the Law Society.We are currently considering advice from our officials in respect of the review report and its recommendations, an associated action plan, the views of interested parties, and next steps. As part of our considerations we also intend to discuss these matters with victim and survivor group representatives when we meet with them shortly.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the production of a new International Relations Strategy, which was expected by summer 2024.
answer_text: Officials are in the process of preparing a draft International Relations Strategy which will form the basis for further engagement and a public consultation.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on free period products legislation.
answer_text: Under section 1 of the Period Products (Free Provision) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, TEO, in partnership with Libraries NI, are providing free period products throughout the Libraries NI network of 96 static and 11 mobile libraries. 				Officials will monitor and evaluate the provision of products going forward and will engage with potential additional delivery partners.				Further to section 2 of the 2022 Act, regulations will be made in due course that will specify the Department’s Public Bodies which will be under a duty to make free products available for use on their premises.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Strategy for Victims and Survivors, which was expected by summer 2024.
answer_text: We will receive an updated Strategy for our consideration shortly, and it is our intention to publish the new Strategy after the summer recess. The new Strategy will be presented to the TEO Committee in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the review of the Race Relations Order, which was expected by summer 2024.
answer_text: A review of the current Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 has been conducted as part of the Racial Equality Strategy 2015 - 2025, including a 12-week public consultation on possible changes which concluded in June 2023. A report on the feedback and responses from the consultation will be published in the coming weeks, however all matters remain subject to Ministerial approval on policy direction. Our intention is to bring updated Race Relations legislation before the Assembly in the 2025-2026 Session.  This will move us a step closer to providing the best possible legal protection against racism.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on their Department's approach to a Framework for Europe, which was a first 100-day commitment.
answer_text: The approach as to how we engage with Europe will be considered as part of the development of the wider International Relations Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the publication of the Community Pharmacy Strategic Plan 2030.
answer_text: I am happy to inform the member that the Department of Health published a new Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy on 7 May 2024. The plan aims to fully realise the potential of community pharmacy services to support better health outcomes from medicines and prevent illness. Implementation of the strategy will not only support elderly and vulnerable patients but the whole population.  The Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy is available on the Department of Health website:  https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/community-pharmacy-strategic-plan-2030This important piece of work was developed collaboratively by the Department of Health and Community Pharmacy Northern Ireland (CPNI), the representative body for community pharmacy owners, and describes the vision and strategic priorities for community pharmacy up to 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on work being carried out to encourage the availability and uptake of blood pressure testing in community pharmacies.
answer_text: I am happy to inform the member that the Department of Health published a new Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy on 7 May 2024. The plan aims to fully realise the potential of community pharmacy services to support better health outcomes from medicines and prevent illness. Implementation of the strategy will not only support elderly and vulnerable patients but the whole population.  The Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy is available on the Department of Health website:  https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/community-pharmacy-strategic-plan-2030One of the priorities is to expand the range of clinical services available through community pharmacies to improve access to patient care pathways for the diagnosis, treatment and prevention of illness.Through the service development work programme to support implementation of the strategy, and subject to securing the necessary funding, new  services will be introduced to meet HSC needs and priorities. A service for hypertension will be considered in that context.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on changes to the drug tariff to ensure community pharmacists are not losing money on them.
answer_text: Following targeted consultation in March 2022, two changes to the Northern Ireland Drug Tariff (NIDT) are currently being implemented in a transitional manner over 18 months with updates as follows:Discounting arrangements: Implementation of the revised discounting arrangements began with the January 2024 prescription payment (made end February ’24). For the first three months of the transitional arrangements, £41,000 less was clawed back from contractors via the revised arrangements than would have been via legacy discount clawback arrangements.Reimbursement of Category A generic medicines in Part 1 NIDTThe objective of this change is to ensure that reimbursement prices better reflect market prices. Part 1 of the NIDT is reflective of the English Drug Tariff and therefore from April 2024, NI are implementing the same transitional arrangements as imposed by DHSC in England.The actual impact of both changes will be closely monitored during the transitional implementation period and consideration will be given to mitigate net material impacts if appropriate. The financial impact on Community Pharmacy of any changes to the NIDT will be monitored via the results of the Northern Ireland Ongoing Margin Survey (OMS) which ensures a minimum threshold of retained purchase profit for community pharmacies.In April 2024, Minister Swann agreed to increase this minimum threshold from £26.5m up to £31.5m along with a move towards new processes in 2024/25 to ensure more timely OMS result reporting.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the funding bids he will be putting forward to enable implementation of the Community Pharmacy Strategic Plan 2030.
answer_text: The Community Pharmacy Strategic Plan brings together four existing inter-related programmes of work led by the Department, to support the delivery of six strategic priorities up to 2030. Those four work programmes relate to: Service Development; Legislative Reform; Workforce; and e-Pharmacy.Some aspects of these programmes are in train and funded, including several initiatives to support workforce development. Also, in April community pharmacy received an increase of £19m to its opening funding position for 2024/25, bringing it to £147m, which represents an increase of 14.8% from the previous year. However, full delivery of the strategic plan is subject to securing additional funding over the next six years, and implementation will be phased. The Department of Health’s budget allocation for 2024-25 represents a 2.3% reduction in funding compared to our expenditure in 2023-24. The 2024-25 opening Budget allocation has left a funding gap of circa £340m.The Department has submitted bids to the Department of Finance for June Monitoring and is aware that there is insufficient funding available to meet our bids.  Due to this position, we are not able to request additional funding for any new work areas – the focus in 2024/25 is on sustaining existing services at current funding levels.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when the funding allocation for Good Relations will be finalised.
answer_text: Allocations across Good Relations Funding Programmes have now been finalised. All organisations have been notified of the outcome of their applications and the distribution of funding is underway.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will publish confirmation of the 2024 funding allocation for (i) central Good Relations funds; (ii) Community Relations Council Core Funding Scheme; and (iii) Council-level Good Relations funds.
answer_text: Allocations across all Good Relations Programmes have now been confirmed for 2024/25. Opening allocations include:£2.275m to the Central Good Relations Fund£2m to the District Council Good Relations Programme£1.392m to the Community Relations Council’s Core Funding Scheme

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether Health and Social Care Trusts are progressing with plans to publish all doctors' rotas, in line with recommended guidance.
answer_text: As you will be aware junior doctors in Northern Ireland are employed under the Single Lead Employer. They work in a number of host HSC organisations, who are responsible for developing, issuing and monitoring the working patterns/ rotas of junior doctors.  Under the current guidance it is recommended that trainees receive their rotas a minimum of 6 weeks prior to taking up post. The NI Medical and Dental Training Agency is responsible for providing the postings to host organisations following the completion of their recruitment campaigns. NIMDTA has confirmed that all allocations for August 2024 have been released to employing organisations with adequate notice to enable HSC Trusts to prepare and issue their rotas. I have been advised by the host organisations (HSC Trusts) that they are making every effort to provide their junior doctors with rotas six weeks prior to the trainees taking up their posts.  They have however, advised that this is not always possible due to vacancies appearing on the postings at late notice.  In cases where the host organisation is unable to provide a rota within the recommended timeframe they state that they advise the trainees, and will where possible provide an indicative rota.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how his Department plans to engage with the newly appointed Belfast City Centre Night Czar.
answer_text: Question 6 - AQO 645/22-27My Department recognises the importance of a thriving night time economy in ensuring our towns and cities are an attractive place to live, work, invest and visit. My Department is currently exploring how it can best support the night time economy. Engaging with relevant stakeholders such as the newly appointed Night Czar for Belfast, Michael Stewart, will be an integral part of this process.  My Department has contacted Michael to arrange a meeting.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how the work plan for the commissioners, appointed under the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022, will be agreed.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail the actions he has taken to secure funding to reinstate school holiday meals payments.
answer_text: In the private member's motion on holiday hunger debated in the Assembly on 19 March 2024, I outlined my view that food poverty is an issue that impacts all age groups on every day of the year and therefore holiday hunger for children should be tackled as part of a wider, more holistic solution to tackling food poverty across society.  Reflecting the close links between tackling food poverty (including holiday hunger), social welfare interventions and the Executive's Anti-Poverty Strategy, I have written to the Minister for Communities seeking to discuss how efforts to tackle food poverty experienced by children could align with the work of the Department for Communities on food poverty and the potential role other departments may have to play in trying to address this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current timescale for when the Glider service will be running along Bankmore Square, Belfast.
answer_text: Officials are currently considering options for delivery of interim measures along the BRT2 route utilising the £35m secured funding in readiness for the introduction of the full Glider Service should the shortfall in funding be met.  These considerations include works in relation to Bankmore Link. My department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues and the Belfast Region City Deal Partners to ensure that the appropriate level of investment is made available to deliver the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of the new Refugee Integration Strategy, whether his Department has made any changes to how it provides culturally-sensitive interpretation services for people accessing GP services for whom English is not their first language.
answer_text: The Department of Health commissions the Business Services Organisation (BSO) to provide an Interpreting service to Health and Social Care providers in Northern Ireland to improve access to HSC services and provide support those who do not speak English proficiently.  BSO is committed to providing a culturally-sensitive service that is responsive to the needs of its service users and is proactively scoping ways of increasing awareness of the service to ensure greater accessibility.  I want to assure the member that the Department of Health has worked extensively with TEO in the development of the Refugee Integration Strategy and the associated delivery plan.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the discussions they have had with the Minister of Health in relation to sensitivities in accessing health services as part of the new Refugee Integration Strategy, including culturally appropriate use of interpreters.
answer_text: Our officials and those from the Department of Health have been in regular contact to inform the development of a Refugee Integration Strategy and associated Thematic Delivery Plan, with one of the themes being ‘Healthcare and Social Care’. Interpretation services, cultural competence and trauma informed practice have been discussed and are being considered for the Thematic Delivery Plan. In addition, the Department of Health commissions the Business Services Organisation (BSO) to provide an interpreting service to Health and Social Care providers here to improve access to HSC services and provide support to those who do not speak English proficiently. BSO is committed to providing a culturally sensitive service that is responsive to the needs of its service users.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to explain the discrepancy between the answers provided to AQW 12959/22-27 and AQW 8075/22-27, including (i) who is now responsible for taking forward the recommendations of the High Street Task Force; and (ii) what actions they are taking to progress these recommendations.
answer_text: We are currently considering a submission from officials regarding the High Street Taskforce Report and appropriate next steps.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when community and voluntary sector organisations will receive an update on the Womens Centres Childcare Fund for the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: Voluntary and Community Sector Groups were notified on 13 June of their indicative allocations for the 2024/25 financial year, which included an uplift equivalent to 5% of their opening allocation last year. These allocations remain indicative as they are subject to the outcome of the ongoing budget consultation exercise.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, given the absence of any published proposals, why officials stated during the Committee for the Executive Office meeting on 5 June 2024 that work on the implementation of the report of the Commission on Flags, Identity, Culture and Tradition was wrapped up.
answer_text: The work of the FICT Commission was completed when they submitted their Report to The Executive Office in July 2020. We are currently considering a submission from officials which includes a range of options for how this work could be taken forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when community and voluntary sector organisations will receive an update on the Community Investment Fund for the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: Voluntary and Community Sector Groups were notified on 13 June of their indicative allocations for the 2024/25 financial year, which included an uplift equivalent to 5% of their opening allocation last year. These allocations remain indicative as they are subject to the outcome of the ongoing budget consultation exercise.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any cuts they have made to the Central Good Relations Fund budget for the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: A budget of £2.275m has been awarded to the Central Good Relations Fund in 2024/25 which has enabled 59 projects to be awarded funding.  This represents an increase in the budget of over 60% compared to 2023/24.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on her plans to increase the minimum age of criminal responsibility.
To ask the Minister for Communities when the Sign Language Bill will be introduced.
answer_text: I have previously stated my commitment for members of the Deaf community to have the same rights and opportunities as those in the hearing community, and therefore my commitment to a Sign Language Bill. To that end, I am currently considering cross-cutting policy proposals to inform the drafting of clauses for a Sign Language Bill which I have included in my Department’s proposed legislative programme for 2024-27 under year 2 – that is September 2024 to July 2025.It is then my intention to present a paper to my Executive colleagues, seeking approval for the drafting of a Sign Language Bill, subject to which I hope to introduce a Bill to the Assembly before the end of this calendar year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the implementation of the recommendations of the Report of the Independent Review of Northern Ireland’s Children’s Social Care Services.
answer_text: The report of the Independent Review of Children’s Social Care Services was published in June 2023 and a public consultation on the Review recommendations ran for 12 weeks between September and December 2023. My officials are in the process of developing advice based on analysis of the responses to the public consultation. Subject to the final outcome to consultation and other factors, including the availability of budget and the views of other Ministers where relevant, the intention would be to implement recommendations as quickly as possible. A number of the recommendations will be taken forward through the Children's Social Care Services Reform Programme as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 10210/22-27.

To ask the Minister of Finance whether a bid has been submitted by the Executive Office for the establishment of any of the three bodies required under the Identity and Language Act 2022 during 2024-25.
To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the establishment of a Public Service Transformation Board.
answer_text: On 9 May, the Executive agreed my proposed approach for making quick progress on the use of £235m of ring-fenced funding for transformation.I have now established, and appointed members to, an interim Public Sector Transformation Board. The interim Board is chaired by the Head of the Civil Service, Jayne Brady, and includes Professor Frances Ruane, an independent Non-Executive member of the NICS Board, and who was previously Director of the Economic and Social Research Institute in Dublin. Julie Harrison, NIO Permanent Secretary, will also join the interim Board having been nominated by the Secretary of State.  The interim Board is working at pace and issued a call for Departments to submit transformation proposals on 31 May. Once assessed, the interim Board will make recommendations to me as Finance Minister.I look forward to considering these recommendations and making progress on the transformation that is so urgently needed in our public services.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the (i) in-person engagement; and (ii) engagement via correspondence, with the Arts Council since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024 in relation to a memorial to the victims and survivors of Historical Institutional Abuse.
answer_text: Officials continue to engage with the Arts Council on memorial plans. We will soon be meeting with victims’ and survivors’ representative groups to hear their views and will consider all relevant proposals for a memorial.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the Executive Office's process for engagement with victims and survivors of Historical Institutional Abuse in relation to a memorial since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: Since the State apology was delivered on 11 March 2022, officials have held several formal meetings with representative group leaders to discuss memorial plans.  Officials have also kept representative group leaders updated on memorial plans informally through regular communication via e-mails and telephone calls.Prior to the restoration of the Executive, official-led engagement events took place in Belfast and the Northwest in November 2023 to discuss victims and survivors’ views on the current working memorial proposal.  Those who did not wish to attend the events, but wished to share their thoughts on the memorial proposals, contacted officials directly to share their views.Ministers met with the Commissioner for Survivors of Institutional Childhood Abuse on 9 April to discuss HIA-related matters.  This included discussions around memorial plans and recommendations from the Commissioner on how this should be taken forward.Ministers will soon be meeting with victims and survivors’ representative groups and will consider all relevant proposals for a memorial.  Officials will continue to engage with, and update, representative group leaders on memorial plans.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when a skilled arts coordinator will be appointed to lead the engagement process with victims and survivors of Historical Institutional Abuse in relation to a memorial.
answer_text: We appreciate that this issue of memorial is sensitive and that there remain widely differing views.  Some victims and survivors wish to see the specific Hart recommendation implemented in full, while others would like to see memorials outside Stormont and Belfast, or the implementation of a living memorial.  We will soon be meeting with victims’ and survivors’ representative groups to hear their views on an HIA memorial.  We will consider all proposals in deciding how memorial plans should be progressed.Officials have continued to engage with the Arts Council as appropriate and will engage further on next steps following the meetings with groups.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how the choice of location of a memorial to victims and survivors of Historical Institutional Abuse in Parliament Buildings was communicated to victims groups.
answer_text: Since the State apology was delivered on 11 March 2022, officials have held several formal meetings with representative group leaders to discuss memorial plans.  Officials have also kept representative group leaders updated on memorial plans through regular communication via e-mails and telephone calls.Engagement events took place in November 2023 to discuss victims’ and survivors’ views on the working proposal for a memorial.  Those who did not wish to attend the events but wished to share their thoughts on the memorial proposals were able to contact officials directly to share their views.  Officials explained that there are a number of challenges associated with a memorial bench in Parliament Buildings, including the building’s listed status and the fact that events are held in the Great Hall which necessitate the removal of benches, which might be considered contrary to the dignity appropriate for a memorial bench. They outlined clearly that the proposal was for a bench in the Rotunda area just outside the Assembly Chamber.We will soon be meeting with victims and survivors representative groups and will consider all relevant proposals for a memorial.  Officials will continue to engage with, and update, representative group leaders on memorial plans.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the budget available for a memorial to the victims and survivors of Historical Institutional Abuse.
answer_text: We will soon be meeting with victims and survivors’ representative groups to hear their views on an HIA memorial.  Once the proposals of victims and survivors have been considered, we will have a clearer idea of the budget required to provide a suitable memorial.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consideration has been given to banning heavy goods vehicles from travelling along Orpen Park and Porter Park, Finaghy, given the safety and noise issues.
answer_text: I can confirm that there are no plans to introduce measures restricting heavy goods vehicles from using Orpen Park and Porter Park, Finaghy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what action his Department is taking to reduce the volume of traffic using Orpen Park, Finaghy, to avoid the Finaghy crossroads.
answer_text: I am aware of the concerns of local residents with regard to the volume of traffic using Orpen Park, Belfast and have asked my officials to undertake an informal consultation,seeking views on proposed traffic movement restrictions into Orpen Park from Finaghy Road South and Upper Lisburn Road during peak traffic flows.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the publication of a new International Relations Strategy.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail the support his Department is offering childcare providers in South Belfast given the rise in demand.
answer_text: My Department continues to progress the development of an Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy as a matter of priority. The affordability and accessibility of childcare for families, alongside other actions to support the early learning and childcare sector, are being considered.  I hope to bring proposals to the Executive in the coming days.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 2359/17-22, to detail the work undertaken by his Department to ensure 30-hour childcare provision.
answer_text: My Department continues to progress the development of an Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy as a matter of priority. The affordability and accessibility of childcare for families, alongside other actions to support the early learning and childcare sector, are being considered.  I hope to bring proposals to the Executive in the coming days.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any consideration has been given to reducing the speed limit on the Ormeau Embankment, given the growth of pedestrian and cycling activity in the area.
answer_text: Ormeau Embankment is subject to a 40mph speed limit, with short sections of 30mph limits on approach to traffic signals at either end. This is in line with the Department’s policy on setting of speed limits.An examination of the recorded road traffic collision history in this area, provided by the PSNI, did not indicate a particular safety concern on Ormeau EmbankmentGiven this information, it is considered that the current speed limit Ormeau Embankment is appropriate and there are no plans to change this.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consideration has been given to the enhancement of (i) road markings; and (ii) lighting on the the Ormeau Embankment, particularly at pedestrian crossings, to improve safety.
answer_text: A recent site inspection carried out by my officials has indicated that all road markings associated with pedestrian crossings on Ormeau Embankment are in good condition and in line with current design standards.My Department also installed a new lighting scheme on Ormeau Embankment, Belfast in February 2018 in accordance with the relevant British Standard. Consequently, there are no plans to carry out any further street lighting works at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will bring forward proposals to make the cycle lanes on the Ormeau Embankment mandatory, rather than optional, thus freeing them from parked cars.
answer_text: The existing cycle lanes along the Ormeau Embankment are mandatory. The Cycle Routes Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 prohibits any vehicle other than a cycle to enter, proceed or wait in these cycle lanes.  The Traffic Management (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 gives the Department the power to issue Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) when a vehicle is detected parked on a legislated Mandatory Cycle Lane. My Department has programmed enforcement in this area in an attempt to change inconsiderate parking behaviour.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he intends to bring forward a strategy to restrict or eliminate the parking of vehicles in cycle lanes.
answer_text: My Department recognises the frustration caused by inconsiderate parking on cycle lanes or cycle tracks and I would encourage motorists to respect everyone’s journey and think before you park.The Traffic Management (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 gives the Department the power to issue Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) when a vehicle is detected parked on a legislated Mandatory Cycle Lane as set out in the Cycle Routes Order (Northern Ireland) 2008. Advisory Cycle Lanes are not enforceable by a Traffic Attendant (TA), however, where parking restrictions are marked on the carriageway, for example yellow lines, those restrictions also apply to the Advisory Cycle Lane.Footway cycle tracks or shared footway/cycleway facilities are not generally enforceable by TAs. If there is a parking restriction marked on the adjacent carriageway, for example yellow lines, this will apply to the adjacent footway and any cycle tracks thereon. Vehicles parked on Advisory Cycle Lanes or footway cycle tracks causing an obstruction, where no parking restrictions are marked on the carriageway, are a matter for the PSNI. Mandatory Cycle Lanes receive regular programmed enforcement.My Department plans to  review the existing legislation with respect to the types of cycling infrastructure that the Department intends to design and construct going forward. If it is determined that a change in legislation would be beneficial in making enforcement of parking in cycle facilities more intuitive and effective this will be brought to the assembly in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the steps they will take to ensure that victims and survivors of workhouses are included in discussions and co-design of actions arising from the work of the Truth Recovery Independent Panel, including (i) legislation on redress; and (ii) an inquiry.
answer_text: Victims and survivors, including those affected by workhouses, played a key role in the co-design process which led to the Truth Recovery Design Panel’s report and recommendations. Their voices continue to inform the programme of implementation of these recommendations, and all victims and survivors have had the opportunity through the ongoing monthly consultation forum to make their views known; including on the nature of policies to inform legislation on the inquiry and redress schemes. Victims and survivors, including those of workhouses, will also have the opportunity to provide testimonies to the Independent Panel, whose members were appointed in April 2023. The Panel’s recommendations will, in turn, help inform the work of the inquiry, as recommended by the Truth Recovery Design Panel’s report.  It is our intention to launch a public consultation on the policy proposals for the statutory public inquiry and redress schemes as quickly as possible, following which we will finalise the draft legislation for Executive agreement prior to introduction in the Assembly. The consultation will provide a further opportunity for victims and survivors of Mother and Baby Institutions, Magdalene Laundries, workhouses and other interested parties to comment in detail on these proposals. We remain committed to ensuring the voices of victims and survivors are clearly heard to inform the policy development process, and would encourage full participation by all interested parties in the forthcoming consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what discussions he has had with counterparts in neighbouring jurisdictions regarding how best to address the impact of intensive poultry production on rivers and waterways across the UK and Ireland.
answer_text: I have had discussions with my counterparts in Ireland on reducing the impacts of the agricultural sector on water quality in rivers and waterways. These discussions will continue, and our officials engage regularly to share experiences and information as we work to improve water quality.In relation to intensive poultry production, significant work has been carried out in Northern Ireland to develop an alternative sustainable outlet for utilising poultry litter and reduce impacts on water quality. My officials have shared the outcome of this work with their counterparts in the UK and Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the work his Department has carried out since May 2022 to address the impact of intensive poultry production on rivers and waterways.
answer_text: Since May 2022 my Department has continued to build on many years of significant work with the poultry industry on the sustainable use of poultry litter.  The establishment of advanced anaerobic digestion facilities, which incorporate nutrient processing technologies, was an outcome of this work. These facilities provide an alternative outlet to the land spreading of poultry litter as an organic fertiliser and contribute to reducing impacts on water quality. My officials also review poultry litter utilisation annually and continue to liaise with the industry on the planned expansion of nutrient processing facilities.My Department continues to have regulatory controls in place to reduce the risk of nutrient pollution of rivers and lakes from agricultural sources. The Nutrients Action Programme (NAP) Regulations and The Pollution Prevention and Control (PPC) Regulations contain measures on the storage, management and spreading of livestock manures, including poultry litter and manure. In 2023, my Department commenced a review of the Nutrients Action Programme which is considering additional measures that are needed to help reduce nutrient losses to waterways from agricultural sources, including the poultry industry.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the aspects of the report of the Commission on Flags, Identity, Culture and Tradition they have progressed since coming to office in February 2024.
answer_text: We refer the Member to the answer provided to AQW 11196/ 22-27.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when the appointments to the three bodies established under the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 will be completed.
answer_text: TEO is taking forward the necessary arrangements to implement the provisions of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (The Act) for which it is responsible, including the establishment of the new bodies, namely: 	•	The Office of Identity and Cultural Expression;	•	The Irish Language Commissioner; and	•	The Commissioner for the Ulster Scots and the Ulster British tradition.This will be in-line with our decisions.Members will be kept informed on progress.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister the detail their agreed approach to the new International Relations Strategy.
answer_text: Since the restoration of the Institutions, our officials have supported official visits from over 25 different countries. We continue to take every opportunity to show that Executive is back up and running and we are open to partnerships that support our economic growth but also help us to deliver outcomes for our citizens.International interest in all that we have to offer continues to grow – we have a reputation as one of the world's best business set-up partners and as an investment destination of choice.The International Relations Strategy will intersect with broader Executive and departmental strategies and actions and will be important in setting out our long-term approach to international engagement. The Strategy requires Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on an agreement in relation to the process for the nomination of Board Members to vacancies on North/South Implementation Bodies.
answer_text: We intend that Executive nominees to vacancies on the Boards of the North South Bodies will be progressed over the forthcoming months and that appointments will be made at NSMC Sectoral meetings.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) when; and (ii) how the discussions on a draft Europe strategy will take place.
answer_text: We will be considering the new International  Relations Strategy and approach shortly, this will include consideration of how we engage with the rest of Europe.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the consultation on the Race Relations Order.
answer_text: A review of the current Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 has been conducted as part of the Racial Equality Strategy 2015 - 2025, including a 12-week public consultation on possible changes which concluded in June 2023. A report on the responses and feedback from the consultation is currently under consideration. We aim to bring updated Race Relations legislation before the Assembly through the legislative programme in the 2025-2026 Session and intend to publish the report in advance of that. The report will inform the basis for any legislative changes and will move us a step closer to providing the best possible legal protection against racism and towards a more equal society.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the support he is providing to the community and independent advice sectors through the Community Support Programme to ensure their services are sustainable.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the specific aspects of the report of the Commission on Flags, Identity, Culture and Tradition they have taken forward since coming into office in February 2024.
answer_text: On 22 April 2024 Officials met with Junior Ministers to discuss matters in relation to the report. Following this, officials are preparing a submission which will set out a number of options for consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the proposals being considered to improve access to Queen's University playing fields, Upper Malone, to ensure improved pedestrian safety and discourage the use of Dub Lane for access.
answer_text: I can advise that access road to and from Queen’s University playing fields on the Upper Malone Road, opposite Bristow Park, is a private entrance, and the current arrangement would have been agreed through the planning process. As such any changes to the entrance would be a matter for Queen’s University to consider.The Department has no proposals to change the current road layout at this location.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they intend to update legislation concerning racial discrimination and racial equality.
answer_text: We are currently planning to bring the updated Race Relations legislation before the Assembly through the legislative programme in the 2025-2026 Session. At present we are considering a report summarising the analysis of the responses and feedback from the consultation process. The report will inform the basis for any legislative changes and will move us a step closer to providing the best possible legal protection against racism.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what assessment his Department has made of the impact of rising utility costs to the viability of amateur sports clubs.
answer_text: My Department has made no formal assessment of the impact of rising utility costs on the viability of amateur sports clubs.  However,  I would refer the member for South Belfast to my responses to her previous questions regarding consideration of Rates Relief for sports clubs (AQW 8318/22-27) and financial support for sports clubs affected by rising utility costs (AQW 8319/22-27) which reference funding support to address utility costs under the Renewable Energy Fund administered by Sport NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the locations being considered for the new Mid Down integrated college.
answer_text: Potential locations for Mid Down integrated college are being considered along the main arterial route which extends from Carryduff to Crossgar.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the date that work to review the International Relations Strategy will be complete.
answer_text: We have seen significant changes internationally in the last 10 years since the first International Relations Strategy was published in 2014.  An updated strategy is needed that reflects our current situation and is agile in its response to developing challenges and opportunities.We recognise the importance of our international presence and engagement with strategic partners and are considering how a future strategy could help to deliver the Executive’s priorities.Our considerations will be based on a significant amount of review work carried out on the existing strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail the long-term financial benefit for repairing roads with tarmac rather than with concrete; and (ii) whether this benefit varies depending on location.
answer_text: Generally concrete surfacing is only used on rigid pavement construction, that is when all pavement layers are concrete. There are very few roads in the North of this type, hence it is not considered a suitable option for maintenance resurfacing on the existing road network.  The use of concrete road surfacing raises environmental concerns including noise and embodied carbon; high initial costs; excessive maintenance costs and high replacement costs at end of life.The vast majority of  our roads are asphaltic / bitmac pavements, which are a flexible pavement construction and potholes in these roads require repair with flexible materials. Concrete is a rigid material which does not bond with the surrounding asphalt material and would result in the breaking up of the surrounding material. Concrete is therefore not a suitable material for use in repairs on the existing road network.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will sign off on the development proposal for Cairnshill Primary School.
answer_text: On 12 October 2023, the Education Authority (EA) published DP 719 at the request of the Board of Governors (BoGs) of Cairnshill PS proposing that:		‘Cairnshill Primary School will transform to Controlled Integrated Status, with effect from 1 September 2024, or as soon as possible thereafter’.	The statutory two-month objection period in relation to this DP ended on 19 December 2023 and I am informed that my officials are preparing a submission for my consideration.  The assessment of any DP is a complex process and requires careful consideration of all evidence to ensure decisions are taken in the best interests of all children and to ensure they have access to high quality education that is viable and sustainable in the long term.I have not yet received a report on the proposal.  I am therefore unable to provide a precise date by which a decision will be taken, but I am aware of the importance of providing clarity to the school and when presented with a report, I will not seek to delay my decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps he is taking to ensure work is progressed urgently on the roof replacement at Cairnshill Primary School.
answer_text: An architect led design team has been appointed for the roof replacement works at Cairnshill Primary School.  It is currently anticipated that design will be completed mid-May, with construction to commence in June.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will sign off on integrated status for Cairnshill Primary School.
answer_text: I refer to my response to AQW 10785/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what meetings he has held since February 2024 with patients, or families of patients, impacted in the Independent Neurology Inquiry.
answer_text: AQW 8320/22-27 details my Department’s response to the Independent Neurology Inquiry (INI) Report and the significant programme of work which has progressed in respect of the INI Implementation Programme. My response to AQW 10600/22-27 outlines the INI Implementation Programme engagement process. This includes the establishment of the Independent Neurology Liaison Group to increase engagement and to focus on the INI Implementation Programme, involving neurology service users and families who have been directly affected by the events which led to the recall of patients of Dr Michael Watt. To date a total of eight Independent Neurology Liaison Group meetings have taken place and a further meeting has been scheduled for 8 May 2024. Four of these meetings have taken place since February 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the reasons for the delays being faced by people trying to exchange an international driving licence to a Northern Ireland driving licence.
answer_text: The Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA) is required to be satisfied that driving licences are issued only to those drivers who meet the relevant requirements in relation to residency and the right to enter or remain in the United Kingdom.The time taken to determine an application where an International Driving licence has been submitted will depend on the country of issue of the licence and the requirements which allow the DVA to exchange or issue a licence to the customer and whether any further information is required as part of the assessment process.  There is currently no delay in processing applications where an International Driving Licence has been submitted for exchange purposes to DVA. The DVA is currently processing these licence applications within two weeks of receipt of a qualifying application where all the relevant paperwork, including proof of residency, has been received.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what proposals he will bring forward to (i) improve; and (ii) speed up the system for people wishing to exchange an international driving licence, including the Channel Islands, for a Northern Ireland driving licence.
answer_text: The Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA) is required to be satisfied that driving licences are issued only to those drivers who meet the relevant requirements in relation to residency and the right to enter or remain in the United Kingdom.The time taken to determine an application where an International Driving licence has been submitted will depend on the country of issue of the licence and the requirements which allow the DVA to exchange or issue a licence to the customer and whether any further information is required as part of the assessment process.  There is currently no delay in processing applications where an International Driving Licence has been submitted for exchange purposes to DVA. The DVA is currently processing these licence applications within two weeks of receipt of a qualifying application where all the relevant paperwork, including proof of residency, has been received.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what meetings he plans to hold in May 2024 with patients, or families of patients, impacted in the Independent Neurology Inquiry.
